title: Regime: 인수 코호트 간 구조적 변화 탐지
text: 장기 건강보험 코호트 포트폴리오를 분석할 때 실무자가 자주 던지는 질문은 두 가지이다. 최근 인수 코호트가 이전 코호트와 다른 양상을 보이는가? 그렇다면 변화는 언제 일어났는가? 장기 보험에서 코호트 패턴이 깨지는 트리거는 보통 다음 4가지다. 급격한 보험료 조정 — 인상 또는 인하 상품 보장 내용 변경 — 보장 항목·기간·면책 등의 구조 조정 가입금액 한도 변경 — 1인당 최대 가입금액 상·하한 조정 Underwriting 가이드라인 변경 — 인수 자격·고지 항목·할증 기준의 개정 번들로 제공되는 experience 데이터셋의 SUR 종목에는 위 트리거 중 하나가 2024-04 시점에 발생했다고 가정한 합성 break 가 심어져 있다. 따라서 아래 시연에서 detect_regime() 이 잡아낼 명확한 변화점이 존재한다. plot(tri_sur) 의 시각적 점검만으로도 최근 코호트의 초기 손해율이 과거보다 낮아 보일 수 있지만, 코호트별로 관측 창의 길이가 다른 상황에서 궤적 다발을 눈대중으로 살피는 것은 구조적 변화의 위치를 짚어내는 신뢰할 만한 방법이 못 된다. detect_regime() 은 이 두 질문에 한 번의 호출로 답한다 — 인수 코호트를 regime (유사한 손해 추이를 공유하는 인수 코호트들의 묶음) 으로 그룹화하고, 그룹 사이의 break 시점을 함께 보고한다. 각 인수 코호트를 특징 벡터 (경과 기간 1, ..., K 에 걸친 궤적) 로 다루고, 인수 시점 순으로 코호트를 정렬한 뒤, 그 다변량 시퀀스에 변화점 또는 클러스터링 방법을 적용한다.

title: Regime: 인수 코호트 간 구조적 변화 탐지
code: library(lossratio)  data(experience) tri_sur <- as_triangle(   experience[coverage == "SUR"],   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" )

title: Regime: 인수 코호트 간 구조적 변화 탐지
text: 기본 방법은 "e_divisive" 로, 데이터로부터 regime 의 개수까지 결정하는 비모수 다변량 변화점 알고리즘이다. window 인자는 코호트 특징 벡터를 정의하는 경과 기간 수를 조절한다. 최소 window 기간 이상 관측된 코호트만 분석되며, 창이 짧은 코호트는 제외된다. window 를 늘리면 궤적을 더 많이 담을 수 있지만 최근 코호트가 그만큼 더 빠진다. 기본값 window = "auto" 는 성숙 기반 sweep 으로 충분한 코호트를 유지하는 최대 window 를 자동 선택한다.
code: r <- detect_regime(tri_sur, method = "e_divisive") r #> <Regime> #>   method    : e_divisive #>   target    : lr #>   treatment : latest_only #>   window (window) : dev_m 1-6 #>   cohorts    : 31 analysed (5 dropped) #>   regimes    : 2 #>   changes    : 24.07 #>   PC1 / PC2  : 80.0% / 12.7%

title: Regime: 인수 코호트 간 구조적 변화 탐지
Regime: 인수 코호트 간 구조적 변화 탐지
text: plot(r) 은 코호트 궤적의 PCA(주성분분석) 산점도를 탐지된 regime 으로 색칠해 보여 준다. PCA 공간에서 regime 들이 잘 분리된다면 구조적 변화가 시각적으로 확인된 것이다.  화살표는 각 경과 기간 특징이 PC 축에 기여하는 적재량을 나타낸다 — regime 들이 어떻게 다른지 (예: 변화가 주로 초기 경과에 영향을 주는지, 후기 경과에 영향을 주는지) 읽어내는 데 유용하다.
code: plot(r)

title: Regime: 인수 코호트 간 구조적 변화 탐지
text: target 인자는 변화점 알고리즘이 어떤 신호 위에서 작동할지 결정한다. target 마다 검출하는 regime 종류가 다르고, 각자 고유한 false positive 모드 가 있다. 의심되는 사건의 성격에 맞춰 target 을 고르고, 결과는 항상 도메인 지식과 대조해야 한다. 순서: c("lr", "loss_ata", "premium_ata", "loss_ed", "premium_ed", "loss", "premium") — cleanest 에서 riskiest 까지. 참고: "lr" 이 default 인 이유 — 손해율이 패키지의 예측 target 이고, 비율이라 book size 성장에 자동 면역. "loss" / "premium" 은 raw cumulative 컬럼이라 갑작스러운 absolute level shift 의심 시 (예: 채널 종료로 보험료 거치액 급감) 유용. smooth book growth 는 false positive 빈번 — 결과를 알려진 언더라이팅·claims 사건 타임라인 과 대조 필수. "loss_ata", "premium_ata", "loss_ed" 는 진단용 target. Triangle 에 저장된 컬럼이 아니라 inline 으로 derive 된다. CL 의 f / ED 의 g 인자와 직접 대응하므로 여기서 검출된 change 는 모델의 stationarity 가정 위반에 해당. 구조적 메커니즘 으로 regime 을 귀속시키고 싶을 때 사용. 진짜 강한 regime shift 는 여러 target 에서 비슷한 change 를 보인다. 신호가 약하거나 book size 성장이 도미넌트일 때 target 간 결과가 갈라진다 — 둘 다 유용한 진단 신호.
code: # 여러 target 을 비교 — 어떤 change 가 일관되게 나오는지 확인 detect_regime(tri_sur, target = "lr") detect_regime(tri_sur, target = "loss") detect_regime(tri_sur, target = "loss_ata")

title: Regime: 인수 코호트 간 구조적 변화 탐지
text: "e_divisive" — 권장 기본값. 다변량, 비모수 알고리즘으로, 주어진 유의수준에서 regime 의 개수를 자동으로 탐지한다. 다른 방법보다 다소 느리지만 사전에 n_regimes 를 정할 필요가 없다. "pelt" — 첫 주성분에 적용되는 빠른 일변량 변화점 탐지. 여러 변화점을 반환할 수 있으며, 궤적 변동이 한 축에 의해 주도될 때 유용하다 (print() 출력의 PC1 % 를 확인 — 70% 초과면 PELT 가 신뢰할 만하고, 그보다 훨씬 낮으면 "e_divisive" 가 낫다). "hclust" — 표준화된 특징 행렬에 Ward 계층 클러스터링을 적용하고 n_regimes 개 (default: 2) 클러스터로 자른다. 시계열 순서를 무시하므로 사후 검증용으로 적합하다. 시계열 기반 방법이 시점 t 에서 변화점을 잡았을 때 hclust 가 동일한 두 그룹 (모든 사전-t 가 한 클러스터, 모든 사후-t 가 다른 클러스터) 을 만들어 낸다면, 이 변화는 방법론적 인공물이 아닌 구조적 변화이다. 실무에서는 세 방법이 모두 일치하는 경우 — 위 SUR 예시처럼 "e_divisive", "pelt", "hclust" 가 모두 24.04 를 regime 경계로 지목하는 경우 — 실제 인수/요율 변경의 강력한 증거가 된다.

title: Regime: 인수 코호트 간 구조적 변화 탐지
text: regime 개수를 고정해 비교하고 싶을 때 — 예를 들어 2-regime 가설과 3-regime 가설을 비교할 때 — n_regimes 를 넘긴다. "e_divisive" 와 "pelt" 의 경우 n_regimes 는 요청값이다 (데이터가 허용하면 알고리즘이 그 수까지 regime 을 반환한다). "hclust" 의 경우에는 강제 컷이다.
code: r2 <- detect_regime(tri_sur, method = "e_divisive", n_regimes = 3) summary(r2) #> Cohort regime detection summary #>   method    : e_divisive #>   target    : lr #>   treatment : latest_only #>   window    : dev_m 1-6 #>   cohorts   : 31 analysed (5 dropped) #>  #> Regimes (3): #>   1: 23.01-23.08 (8 cohorts) #>   2: 23.09-24.06 (10 cohorts) #>   3: 24.07-25.07 (13 cohorts) #>  #> Changes: 23.09, 24.07

title: Regime: 인수 코호트 간 구조적 변화 탐지
text: 여러 그룹으로 구축한 Triangle 은 그대로 detect_regime() 에 전달할 수 있다. 그룹별로 독립적으로 탐지가 수행되며 결과는 하나의 Regime 객체에 모인다. 다중 그룹 모드에서 r_all$changes 는 그룹 컬럼과 change Date 컬럼을 가진 data.table 이고, r_all$labels 에도 그룹 컬럼이 추가된다. r_all$n_regimes 는 그룹값을 이름으로 하는 정수 벡터이며, r_all$multi_group 플래그가 단일 그룹 스칼라 형식과 구분해준다. 특정 그룹의 코호트 수가 window 보다 적으면 그 그룹은 경고와 함께 skip 되고 나머지 그룹은 정상 진행된다. 모든 그룹이 실패하면 detect_regime() 은 오류를 발생시킨다. plot(r_all) 은 그룹별 패널 ggplot 객체의 named list 를 반환한다 (그룹값을 key 로).
code: tri_all <- as_triangle(   experience,   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" ) r_all   <- detect_regime(tri_all, by = "coverage", method = "e_divisive") r_all$changes #>    coverage     change regime_id pre_value post_value magnitude #>      <char>     <Date>     <int>     <num>      <num>     <num> #> 1:      SUR 2024-07-01         2  1.041267  0.5968553 0.4444118

title: Regime: 인수 코호트 간 구조적 변화 탐지
text: detect_regime() 은 fit_lr() 프레임워크의 수정이 아니라 전처리 진단 이다. 그 출력은 두 가지로 활용된다. 층화 적합: 명확히 구분되는 두 regime 이 탐지된 경우, 각 regime 부분집합에 대해 fit_lr() 을 따로 적합하면 풀링 적합보다 더 또렷한 수렴 영역의 손해율 추정값을 얻는 경우가 많다. 요율 변경 문서화: 탐지된 변화점은 동반 논문의 Limitations 절에서 설명한 전처리 권고 (보험료 -leveling 또는 익스포저 분해 V=CP/rV = C^P / r) 의 데이터 기반 기준점이 된다.

title: Regime: detecting structural breaks across underwriting cohorts
text: analysing portfolio long-duration health insurance cohorts, practitioner often asks two questions: recent underwriting cohorts behaving differently earlier cohorts? , change happen? long-term insurance, cohort patterns commonly break one four triggers: Drastic premium adjustment — large - -revision rates Product coverage content change — restructuring benefits, exclusions, term Sum insured limit change — adjustment per-policy maximum Underwriting guideline change — eligibility, declarations, loading rule revisions bundled experience dataset’s SUR coverage carries synthetic 2024-04 break representing one triggers, demonstration clear shift detect_regime() find. visual inspection plot(tri_sur) can suggest recent cohorts lower early loss ratios older ones, eye-balling bundle trajectories unreliable way locate structural shift — especially observation windows differ across cohorts. detect_regime() answers questions one call — grouping underwriting cohorts regimes (groups cohorts share similar loss dynamics) reporting break dates groups. treats underwriting cohort feature vector (trajectory first window development periods), orders cohorts underwriting date, applies change-point clustering method resulting multivariate sequence.

title: Regime: detecting structural breaks across underwriting cohorts
code: library(lossratio)  data(experience) tri_sur <- as_triangle(   experience[coverage == "SUR"],   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" )

title: Regime: detecting structural breaks across underwriting cohorts
text: default method "e_divisive", non-parametric multivariate change-point algorithm determines number regimes data: window argument controls many development periods define cohort feature vector. cohorts observed least window periods analysed; cohorts shorter windows dropped. Increasing window captures trajectory drops recent cohorts. default window = "auto", maturity-aware sweep picks largest window retains enough cohorts.
code: r <- detect_regime(tri_sur, method = "e_divisive") r #> <Regime> #>   method    : e_divisive #>   target    : lr #>   treatment : latest_only #>   window (window) : dev_m 1-6 #>   cohorts    : 31 analysed (5 dropped) #>   regimes    : 2 #>   changes    : 24.07 #>   PC1 / PC2  : 80.0% / 12.7%

Regime: detecting structural breaks across underwriting cohorts
text: plot(r) produces PCA scatter cohort trajectories coloured detected regime. regimes well-separated PCA space, structural shift visually confirmed:  Arrows indicate loadings development-period feature PC axes — useful reading regimes differ (e.g. whether shift primarily affects early late development).
code: plot(r)

title: Regime: detecting structural breaks across underwriting cohorts
text: target argument controls signal change-point algorithm operates . Different targets surface different kinds regime change, failure mode. Pick target matches event suspect — always cross-check domain knowledge. Order: c("lr", "loss_ata", "premium_ata", "loss_ed", "premium_ed", "loss", "premium") — cleanest riskiest. Notes: "lr" default loss ratio package’s projection target naturally scale-invariant (immune book-size growth). "loss" / "premium" use raw cumulative columns useful suspected event sudden absolute level shift (e.g. channel termination dropping premium volume). Smooth book growth frequently produce false positives — read every result alongside known timeline underwriting / claims-handling events. "loss_ata", "premium_ata", "loss_ed" diagnostic targets derived inline (stored Triangle). map directly CL f-factor / ED g-factor used fitting, change detected corresponds violation model’s stationarity assumption. Use want attribute regime change specific structural mechanism. changes across targets usually similar strong, real regime shifts diverge signal weak driven book-size growth — useful diagnostics.
code: # Try several targets and compare the changes they surface detect_regime(tri_sur, target = "lr") detect_regime(tri_sur, target = "loss") detect_regime(tri_sur, target = "loss_ata")

title: Regime: detecting structural breaks across underwriting cohorts
text: "e_divisive" — preferred default. Multivariate, non-parametric, auto-detects number regimes given significance level. Slightly slower alternatives requires priori choice n_regimes. "pelt" — fast univariate change-point detection applied first principal component. May return multiple changes useful trajectory variation dominated one axis (check PC1 % print() output — > 70%, PELT reliable; much lower, prefer "e_divisive"). "hclust" — Ward hierarchical clustering scaled feature matrix, cut n_regimes clusters (default 2). Ignores chronological order best used sanity check: chronological methods locate change time t hclust produces two groups (pre-t one cluster, post-t ), shift structural rather artefact method. practice, agreement across three methods — SUR example , "e_divisive", "pelt", "hclust" locate 24.04 regime boundary — strong evidence real underwriting/rate shift.

title: Regime: detecting structural breaks across underwriting cohorts
text: want compare fixed number regimes — example, two-vs-three regime hypotheses — pass n_regimes: "e_divisive" "pelt", n_regimes request (algorithm return many regimes supported data). "hclust", hard cut.
code: r2 <- detect_regime(tri_sur, method = "e_divisive", n_regimes = 3) summary(r2) #> Cohort regime detection summary #>   method    : e_divisive #>   target    : lr #>   treatment : latest_only #>   window    : dev_m 1-6 #>   cohorts   : 31 analysed (5 dropped) #>  #> Regimes (3): #>   1: 23.01-23.08 (8 cohorts) #>   2: 23.09-24.06 (10 cohorts) #>   3: 24.07-25.07 (13 cohorts) #>  #> Changes: 23.09, 24.07

title: Regime: detecting structural breaks across underwriting cohorts
text: Triangle built multiple groups can passed directly — detection runs independently per group results gathered single Regime object. multi-group mode r_all$changes data.table group column plus change Date column; r_all$labels likewise gains group column; r_all$n_regimes named integer vector keyed group value. r_all$multi_group flag distinguishes layout single-group scalar form. group cohorts chosen window, group skipped warning (others continue). groups fail, detect_regime() errors . plot(r_all) returns named list per-group ggplot panels (keyed group value).
code: tri_all <- as_triangle(   experience,   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" ) r_all   <- detect_regime(tri_all, by = "coverage", method = "e_divisive") r_all$changes #>    coverage     change regime_id pre_value post_value magnitude #>      <char>     <Date>     <int>     <num>      <num>     <num> #> 1:      SUR 2024-07-01         2  1.041267  0.5968553 0.4444118

title: Regime: detecting structural breaks across underwriting cohorts
text: detect_regime() preprocessing diagnostic, modification fit_lr() framework. output useful two ways: Stratified fitting: two clearly distinct regimes detected, fitting fit_lr() separately regime subset often yields sharper stable-CLR estimates pooled fit. Rate-change documentation: detected change provides data-driven anchor preprocessing recommendations outlined Limitations section companion paper (premium -leveling exposure decomposition V = C^P / r).

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: 이 문서는 간결성을 위해 SUR 그룹만 사용한다 — 모든 절차는 다중 그룹 입력에도 그대로 일반화된다.
code: library(lossratio) data(experience) exp <- experience[coverage == "SUR"] tri <- as_triangle(   exp,   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" )

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: summary = TRUE overlay 는 각 dev 에서 평균, 중앙값, 가중 lr 을 계산해 코호트 선 위에 겹쳐 그린다. 중심 경향에서 벗어나는 코호트를 포착하는 데 유용하다.
code: plot(tri)                              # 코호트별 누적 손해율 궤적 plot(tri, metric = "lr_incr")       # 누적 대신 증분 손해율 plot(tri, summary = TRUE)              # 코호트 선 + overlay (평균 / 중앙값 / 가중)

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
code: plot_triangle(tri, metric = "lr")          # 누적 lr plot_triangle(tri, metric = "lr_incr")     # 증분 lr # detail 라벨은 두 줄이라 monthly 셀에서는 겹침 — quarterly 로 다시 빌드 tri_q <- as_triangle(exp, groups = "coverage", cohort = "uy_m", calendar = "cy_m", loss = "loss_incr", premium = "premium_incr", grain = "Q") plot_triangle(tri_q, label_style = "detail")  # 비율 + (loss / premium)

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: (group, dev) 셀별 평균 / 중앙값 / 가중 손해율을 담은 TriangleSummary 객체를 반환한다.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: Link 객체는 triangle 으로부터 빌드된 단계 연결 테이블(link table) 이다. 단일 변수 모드에서는 관측된 ATA 인자(age--age factor) 를 담고, exposure 를 지정하면 ED 강도 gk=ΔCkL/CkPg_k = \Delta C^L_k / C^P_k 를 담는다. Link 객체 (단일 변수 모드) 의 summary() 메소드는 성숙점 탐지를 구동하는 링크별 통계를 계산한다. mean, median, wt — 각 링크에서 관측된 ATA 인자의 기술 평균 (해당 링크가 관측되지 않은 코호트는 제외). cv — 관측 인자의 변동계수 (상대 산포, alpha 와 무관). f — WLS 로 추정된 인자 (loss_from^alpha 로 볼륨 가중). f_se, rse — WLS 표준오차 및 상대 표준오차. sigma — 링크별 Mack 잔차 sigma. n_cohorts, n_valid, n_inf, n_nan, valid_ratio — 관측 수와 링크별 유한 ATA 인자의 비율.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
code: plot(ata, type = "cv")            # ata 링크별 CV (성숙점 overlay 포함) plot(ata, type = "rse")           # ata 링크별 RSE plot(ata, type = "summary")       # 링크별 mean / median / wt overlay plot(ata, type = "box")           # 링크별 관측 ata 의 boxplot plot(ata, type = "point")         # 링크별 관측 ata 의 산점도

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: 이 히트맵은 각 셀을 자기 링크 내에서 log(ata / median(ata)) 로 색칠하므로, 열 방향 색상은 해당 링크의 중앙값에서 벗어나는 코호트를 구분해 준다.
code: plot_triangle(ata, label_size = 2.5)              # 관측 인자 히트맵 plot_triangle(ata, label_size = 2.5, show_maturity = TRUE)  # 성숙점 라인 overlay # detail 라벨은 두 줄이라 monthly 셀에서는 겹침 — quarterly Link 로 다시 빌드 ata_q <- as_link(tri_q, target = "loss") plot_triangle(ata_q, label_style = "detail")      # 인자 + (loss / premium)

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: summary(link, model = "ed") 는 단일 변수 summary() 에 대응하는 ED 측 분석으로, 강도 gk=ΔCkL/CkPg_k = \Delta C^L_k / C^P_k 에 대해 링크별 통계를 계산한다.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: 성숙점(maturity point) 은 ATA 인자(age--age factor) 가 chain ladder 추정에 신뢰할 만큼 안정화되는 경과 기간 링크이다. fit_lr(method = "sa") 가 ED 에서 CL 로 전환할 때 내부적으로 사용한다.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: detect_maturity() 는 Triangle 을 직접 입력으로 받는다 — 내부에서 단일 변수 Link 와 그 WLS 요약을 자동으로 빌드한다. 그룹별로 모든 임계값을 만족하는 첫 경과 기간 링크 한 행이 출력되며, 해당 링크의 전체 통계가 같이 실린다. 임계값 인자들은 반환된 Maturity 객체의 attribute 로도 저장된다.
code: mat <- detect_maturity(   tri,   target          = "loss",   max_cv          = 0.15,    # CV 가 이 값보다 작아야 함   max_rse         = 0.05,    # RSE 가 이 값보다 작아야 함   min_valid_ratio = 0.5,     # 해당 링크에서 유한 코호트가 50% 이상   min_n_valid     = 3L,      # 유한 코호트가 최소 3개   min_run         = 1L       # 연속 성숙 링크 최소 1개 )  print(mat) #> Key: <coverage> #>    coverage ata_from change ata_link     mean   median       wt        cv #>      <char>    <num>  <num>   <char>    <num>    <num>    <num>     <num> #> 1:      SUR        3      4      3-4 1.434507 1.400098 1.417706 0.1053282 #>           f       f_se        rse    sigma n_cohorts n_valid n_inf n_nan #>       <num>      <num>      <num>    <num>     <num>   <num> <num> <num> #> 1: 1.417706 0.02651852 0.01870522 1372.883        33      33     0     0 #>    valid_ratio #>          <num> #> 1:           1

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: max_cv — 해당 링크에서 관측된 ATA 인자의 변동계수. alpha 와 무관하게 상대 산포를 제한한다. max_rse — WLS 추정 인자 f 의 상대 표준오차. 잔차 산포가 아니라 파라미터 불확실성을 포착한다. min_valid_ratio — 해당 링크에서 유한 ATA 를 갖는 코호트의 최소 비율. 대부분이 0 / NA / Inf 인 링크를 막는다. min_n_valid — 해당 링크에서 유한 코호트의 최소 개수. 데이터가 얇은 꼬리 영역의 절대 하한. min_run — 연속 성숙 링크의 최소 개수. min_run = 1L (default) 이면 조건을 만족하는 첫 링크가 채택되고, 2L 이상으로 두면 지속적인 안정성을 요구한다. 포트폴리오의 변동성 프로파일에 맞춰 조정한다. 임계값을 빡빡하게 (예: max_cv = 0.05) 잡으면 성숙점이 뒤로 밀리고, 느슨하게 잡으면 앞으로 당겨진다.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: detect_maturity() 는 fit_ata() 와 fit_cl() 내부에서도 maturity 인자를 통해 호출된다. 사용자 임계값을 넘기려면 maturity_spec() 으로 감싸 전달하고, default 값으로 자동 탐지하려면 "auto" 를 쓴다 (fit_lr(method = "sa") / fit_loss() 는 default 가 "auto"). maturity 인자는 네 가지 형태를 받는다: NULL (탐지 안 함), 이미 산출된 Maturity 객체 (detect_maturity() 결과 또는 수동 override 용 maturity_at()), 문자열 "auto" (default 임계값으로 내부 탐지), 또는 triangle 한 개를 인자로 받아 Maturity 를 반환하는 함수 (보통 maturity_spec() 로 작성). fit_lr(method = "sa") 에서는 탐지된 성숙점이 ED (초기 dev) 에서 CL (이후 dev) 로 전환되는 dev 를 결정한다.
code: fit_ata(tri, target = "loss",         maturity = maturity_spec(max_cv = 0.08, min_run = 2L))  fit_cl(tri, target = "loss",        maturity = maturity_spec(max_cv = 0.08))  fit_lr(tri, method = "sa",        maturity = maturity_spec(max_cv = 0.08))

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: 다중 그룹 triangle 의 경우 detect_maturity() 는 그룹별로 한 행씩 반환하며, 각 그룹은 동일한 임계값 하에서 독립적으로 탐지된다. 링크 진단 플롯으로 보면 결과를 한눈에 읽기 쉽다 — cv 별 facet 으로 나뉘고, 수직선이 CV 가 max_cv 아래로 처음 떨어지는 성숙점을 표시한다.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: 경과 기간 시퀀스에 결손이 의심되면 as_triangle() 호출 전에 점검한다. 경과 기간이 비연속인 코호트마다 한 행씩을 담은 TriangleValidation 객체를 반환한다. 결과가 비어 있다면 triangle 이 깨끗하다는 뜻이다. 결손이 있는 경우의 선택지는 다음과 같다. 데이터 원본을 수정한다 (권장). 문제가 있는 코호트를 제외한다. as_triangle() 에 fill_gaps = TRUE 를 넘겨 누락 셀을 0 으로 채운다 (단, n_cohorts 가 실제 관측 개수보다 많아지므로 신중히 사용).
code: gaps <- validate_triangle(exp, groups = "coverage", cohort = "uy_m", development = "dev_m") head(gaps) #> <TriangleValidation> #> Cohort dev-sequence gaps : none

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: 오래된 코호트가 더 이상 대표성이 없을 때 (요율 변경, 인수 가이드라인 변경 등) 추정을 최근 대각선으로 제한한다. recent = K 는 calendar 위치 (rank(cohort) + dev - 1) 가 그룹 내 최근 K 개에 속하는 행만 남긴다.
code: fit_ata(tri, target = "loss", alpha = 1, recent = 12)  # 최근 12개 대각선 #> <ATAFit> #> alpha       : 1  #> sigma_method: locf  #> recent      : 12  #> regime      : none #> use_maturity: FALSE  #> groups      : coverage  #> n_groups    : 1  #> ata links   : 35 fit_cl(tri, target = "loss", recent = 12) #> <CLFit> #> method      : mack  #> target      : loss  #> weight      : none  #> alpha       : 1  #> sigma_method: locf  #> recent      : 12  #> regime      : none #> use_maturity: FALSE  #> tail_factor : 1  #> groups      : coverage  #> periods     : 36 fit_lr(tri, recent = 12) #> <LRFit> #> method         : sa  #> loss_alpha     : 1  #> premium_alpha  : 1  #> se_method      : fixed  #> conf_level     : 0.95  #> ci_type        : analytical   #> sigma_method   : locf  #> recent         : 12  #> loss_regime    : none #> premium_regime : none #> maturity[SUR]  : 4  #> groups         : coverage  #> periods        : 36

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: 적합 전에 확인할 사항은 다음과 같다. validate_triangle() — 스키마와 결손 점검. as_triangle() — 파생 컬럼이 포함된 표준 형태 구축. plot(tri) / plot_triangle(tri) — 시각적 점검. summary(tri) — 그룹 수준 중심 경향 확인. as_link() + plot(link, type = "cv") — 링크 안정성 확인. detect_maturity() — 그룹별로 합리적 성숙점이 잡히는지 확인. detect_regime() (선택) — 구조적 변화 진단. 이후 신뢰할 수 있는 입력 데이터로 fit_lr() / fit_cl() 을 적합한다.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: 데이터 구조와 factor 진단
text: vignette("getting-started") — 전체 파이프라인 개요. vignette("regime") — detect_regime() 심화. vignette("projection") — 추정 방법 선택.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: brevity vignette uses SUR group — every step generalises multi-group input.
code: library(lossratio) data(experience) exp <- experience[coverage == "SUR"] tri <- as_triangle(   exp,   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" )

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: summary = TRUE overlay computes mean, median, weighted lr dev overlays cohort lines. Useful spotting cohorts deviate central tendency.
code: plot(tri)                              # cumulative loss-ratio trajectories per cohort plot(tri, metric = "lr_incr")       # incremental loss ratio instead of cumulative plot(tri, summary = TRUE)              # raw + overlay (mean / median / weighted)

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
code: plot_triangle(tri, metric = "lr")          # cumulative lr plot_triangle(tri, metric = "lr_incr")     # incremental lr # detail labels (ratio + loss/premium amounts) are 2-line — use quarterly cells tri_q <- as_triangle(exp, groups = "coverage", cohort = "uy_m", calendar = "cy_m", loss = "loss_incr", premium = "premium_incr", grain = "Q") plot_triangle(tri_q, label_style = "detail") # ratio + (loss / premium)

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: Returns TriangleSummary object mean / median / weighted loss ratios per (group, dev) cell.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: Link object link table (age--age factor table) built triangle. single-variable mode carries observed ATA factors; exposure carries ED-style intensities gk=ΔCkL/CkPg_k = \Delta C^L_k / C^P_k. summary() method Link object (single-variable mode) computes per-link statistics drive maturity detection: mean, median, wt — descriptive averages observed ata factors link (excluding cohorts link observed). cv — coefficient variation observed factors (relative spread, alpha-independent). f — WLS-estimated factor (volume-weighted loss_from^alpha). f_se, rse — WLS standard error relative standard error. sigma — Mack residual sigma per link. n_cohorts, n_valid, n_inf, n_nan, valid_ratio — observation counts share finite ATA factors per link.

what: Diagnostic plots for the link table
title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
code: plot(ata, type = "cv")            # CV vs ata link with maturity overlay plot(ata, type = "rse")           # RSE vs ata link plot(ata, type = "summary")       # mean / median / wt overlay per link plot(ata, type = "box")           # boxplot of observed ata per link plot(ata, type = "point")         # scatter of observed ata per link

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: heatmap colours cell log(ata / median(ata)) within link, column-wise colour distinguishes cohorts deviate link’s median.
code: plot_triangle(ata, label_size = 2.5)              # heatmap of observed factors plot_triangle(ata, label_size = 2.5, show_maturity = TRUE)   # overlay maturity line # detail labels are two lines and overlap on monthly cells — rebuild on # the quarterly triangle so the two-line "factor (loss / premium)" text # has room (label_size auto-shrinks to 2.2 in detail mode). ata_q <- as_link(tri_q, target = "loss") plot_triangle(ata_q, label_style = "detail")      # factor + (loss / premium)

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: summary(link, model = "ed") ED-side analogue single-variable summary(), computing per-link statistics intensity gk=ΔCkL/CkPg_k = \Delta C^L_k / C^P_k.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: maturity point development link beyond age--age factors stable enough trust chain-ladder projection. Used internally fit_lr(method = "sa") switch ED CL.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: detect_maturity() takes Triangle directly — underlying single-variable Link WLS summary built internally: row per group first development link satisfying thresholds, carrying link’s full statistics. threshold arguments also stored attributes returned Maturity object.
code: mat <- detect_maturity(   tri,   target          = "loss",   max_cv          = 0.15,    # CV must be below this   max_rse         = 0.05,    # RSE must be below this   min_valid_ratio = 0.5,     # at least 50% finite cohorts at the link   min_n_valid     = 3L,      # at least 3 finite cohorts   min_run         = 1L       # at least 1 consecutive mature link )  print(mat) #> Key: <coverage> #>    coverage ata_from change ata_link     mean   median       wt        cv #>      <char>    <num>  <num>   <char>    <num>    <num>    <num>     <num> #> 1:      SUR        3      4      3-4 1.434507 1.400098 1.417706 0.1053282 #>           f       f_se        rse    sigma n_cohorts n_valid n_inf n_nan #>       <num>      <num>      <num>    <num>     <num>   <num> <num> <num> #> 1: 1.417706 0.02651852 0.01870522 1372.883        33      33     0     0 #>    valid_ratio #>          <num> #> 1:           1

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: max_cv — coefficient variation observed ATA factors link. Caps relative spread regardless alpha. max_rse — relative standard error WLS-estimated factor f. Captures parameter uncertainty rather residual spread. min_valid_ratio — minimum share cohorts finite ATA link. Guards links observations zero / NA / Inf. min_n_valid — minimum count finite cohorts link. Absolute floor thin-data tails. min_run — minimum number consecutive mature links. min_run = 1L (default) first qualifying link wins; setting 2L higher requires sustained stability. Tune portfolio’s volatility profile. Tight thresholds (e.g. max_cv = 0.05) push maturity later; loose thresholds push earlier.

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: detect_maturity() also called internally fit_ata() fit_cl() via maturity argument. Pass maturity_spec() forward custom detection thresholds, "auto" defaults (fit_lr(method = "sa") fit_loss() use "auto" default): maturity argument accepts four forms: NULL (detection), pre-built Maturity object (e.g. detect_maturity() maturity_at() manual override), string "auto" (internal detect_maturity() defaults), function one triangle argument returning Maturity (typically built maturity_spec()). fit_lr(method = "sa") detected maturity point determines dev projection switches ED (early dev) CL (later dev).
code: fit_ata(tri, target = "loss",         maturity = maturity_spec(max_cv = 0.08, min_run = 2L))  fit_cl(tri, target = "loss",        maturity = maturity_spec(max_cv = 0.08))  fit_lr(tri, method = "sa",        maturity = maturity_spec(max_cv = 0.08))

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: gaps development sequence suspected, inspect as_triangle(): Returns TriangleValidation object one row per cohort non-consecutive development periods. empty result means triangle clean. gaps exist, options: Fix data source (preferred). Drop offending cohorts. Pass fill_gaps = TRUE as_triangle() zero-fill missing cells (use care — inflates n_cohorts).
code: gaps <- validate_triangle(exp, groups = "coverage", cohort = "uy_m", development = "dev_m") head(gaps) #> <TriangleValidation> #> Cohort dev-sequence gaps : none

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics
text: older cohorts longer representative (rate change, reserving regime shift), restrict estimation recent calendar diagonals: recent = K keeps rows whose calendar position (rank(cohort) + dev - 1) among latest K per group.
code: fit_ata(tri, target = "loss", alpha = 1, recent = 12)  # last 12 calendar diagonals #> <ATAFit> #> alpha       : 1  #> sigma_method: locf  #> recent      : 12  #> regime      : none #> use_maturity: FALSE  #> groups      : coverage  #> n_groups    : 1  #> ata links   : 35 fit_cl(tri, target = "loss", recent = 12) #> <CLFit> #> method      : mack  #> target      : loss  #> weight      : none  #> alpha       : 1  #> sigma_method: locf  #> recent      : 12  #> regime      : none #> use_maturity: FALSE  #> tail_factor : 1  #> groups      : coverage  #> periods     : 36 fit_lr(tri, recent = 12) #> <LRFit> #> method         : sa  #> loss_alpha     : 1  #> premium_alpha  : 1  #> se_method      : fixed  #> conf_level     : 0.95  #> ci_type        : analytical   #> sigma_method   : locf  #> recent         : 12  #> loss_regime    : none #> premium_regime : none #> maturity[SUR]  : 4  #> groups         : coverage  #> periods        : 36

title: Triangle, Link and Maturity: data structures and factor diagnostics

title: lossratio
text: Loss ratio analytics long-term health insurance — cohort development analysis, stage-adaptive projection, regime detection, backtest validation.

title: lossratio
text: lossratio loss ratio analytics toolkit long-term health insurance, covering cohort development analysis, stage-adaptive projection, regime detection, backtest validation. Input long-format experience data — row (cohort × dev × demographic) maps one Triangle cell, loss premium columns (loss, premium). long-term health insurance, new claims premium generated earned continuously within cohort, cumulative loss exposure grow together. Age--age (ATA) factors tend show high variability early development, exposure (≈ risk premium) becomes stable reliable anchor. Product redesigns, underwriting changes, regulatory actions can also produce structural breaks accumulate across cohorts. setting, lossratio provides stage-adaptive (SA) loss-ratio projection, supported maturity point regime detection. SA uses exposure-driven (ED) model maturity point chain ladder (CL) . Regime detection identifies homogeneous groups cohorts (regimes) share similar loss dynamics, separating structural break points determining cells use estimation. provides: Three aggregation frameworks experience data: cohort × dev (Triangle), calendar period (Calendar), portfolio total (Total) Age--age (ATA) exposure-driven (ED) development modeling via worker layer (fit_cl, fit_ed, fit_ata, fit_intensity) Role-specific dispatchers (fit_loss, fit_premium) project single side standard errors confidence intervals "sa" — stage-adaptive (default): exposure-driven maturity, chain ladder "ed" — exposure-driven development periods "cl" — classical chain ladder (Mack model) Standard errors via se_method = "fixed" (premium treated known) "delta" (delta method L / P); CIs via conf_level; bootstrap option empirical CIs. detect_maturity — dev axis: link beyond ATA factors stable detect_regime — cohort axis: structural breaks across underwriting detect_convergence — valuation vv projected ultimate loss ratio stops revising (operates fitted LRFit) Backtest triangle visualisations

title: lossratio
text: long-format data.frame / data.table minimum: as_triangle() validates schema, coerces date columns, aggregates canonical cohort × dev structure cumulative columns derived ratios.

title: lossratio
text: Throughout package, cumulative unmarked default per-period values carry _incr (incremental) suffix: Raw experience input per-period (loss_incr, premium_incr); as_triangle() produces forms output. Worker fit functions (fit_cl, fit_ed, fit_ata, fit_intensity) take generic target / exposure / weight arguments; dispatcher functions (fit_loss, fit_premium) composition fit_lr use role-specific loss_* / premium_* argument names. Cumulative slots ("loss", "premium") defaults.

title: lossratio
text: package currently depends seokhoonj/instead seokhoonj/ggshort (installed automatically via Remotes:; planned removal future release).
code: # pak (recommended) pak::pak("seokhoonj/lossratio")  # remotes (alternative) remotes::install_github("seokhoonj/lossratio")

title: lossratio
code: library(lossratio)  # Built-in calibrated synthetic experience data # (per-coverage dev curve calibrated to a real portfolio's broad shape; # cell-level values and cohort patterns are randomly generated) data(experience)  # Build the canonical cohort × dev structure tri <- as_triangle(   experience,   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" )  plot(tri)              # cohort trajectories plot_triangle(tri)     # cell heatmap  # Exposure-driven fit (additive ED intensity) ed <- fit_ed(tri, target = "loss", exposure = "premium")  # Chain ladder fit (multiplicative ATA factors) cl <- fit_cl(tri, target = "loss") plot(cl, type = "projection")  # Loss ratio fit (stage-adaptive by default — ED before maturity, CL after) lr <- fit_lr(tri, method = "sa") plot(lr, metric = "lr", cell_type = "cumulative") summary(lr)  # Cell selection: maturity (dev axis) + regime (cohort axis) detect_maturity(tri[coverage == "SUR"]) detect_regime(tri[coverage == "SUR"], method = "e_divisive")  # Projection diagnostic: when does the projected ultimate LR stop revising? detect_convergence(lr)

title: lossratio
text: long-format experience data can viewed three ways: as_triangle, downstream columns standardized cohort dev regardless input granularity (uy_m / uy_q / uy, etc.). Original column names preserved attributes (cohort, calendar, dev); grain stored grain ("M"/"Q"/ "H"/"Y").

title: lossratio
text: fit_lr(method = "sa") (default). Hybrid exposure-driven chain ladder, switching maturity point per group: maturity: exposure-driven projection ΔCL=gk⋅CkP\Delta C^L = g_k \cdot C^P_k — anchors estimate premium volume age--age factors volatile. maturity: chain ladder projection Ck+1L=fk⋅CkLC^L_{k+1} = f_k \cdot C^L_k — preserves cohort’s observed level age--age factors stabilise.

title: lossratio
text: fit_lr(method = "ed"). future loss increments use exposure (risk premium) denominator. Suitable age--age factors uninformative unstable across full development.

title: lossratio
text: fit_lr(method = "cl") fit_cl(). Classical Mack chain ladder optional log-linear tail factor analytic Mack standard errors.

what: Coerce experience data to a Calendar object — as_calendar
title: Coerce experience data to a Calendar object — as_calendar
text: Validate raw experience data, aggregate along single calendar-period axis, assign Calendar S3 class associated plot.Calendar() / summary.Calendar() / longer-form methods dispatch result. Compared as_triangle(), builds two-dimensional cohort × dev structure, as_calendar() one-dimensional: single calendar-period time series (per group) showing portfolio evolves time, regardless cohort membership. result long-format data.table class c("Calendar", "data.table", "data.frame") containing cumulative loss / premium, incremental cumulative LR, margin, profit, share columns within calendar cell. cumulative loss ratio defined : $$lr = loss / premium$$ long-term health insurance applications, risk premium commonly used premium measure. Proportion variables computed within calendar cell: loss_incr_share    = loss_incr    / sum(loss_incr) premium_incr_share = premium_incr / sum(premium_incr) loss_share         = loss         / sum(loss) premium_share      = premium      / sum(premium) Therefore, fixed calendar cell, proportions sum 1 across groups. useful examining composition calendar period across products grouping variables.

title: Coerce experience data to a Calendar object — as_calendar
code: as_calendar(   df,   groups = NULL,   calendar,   loss,   premium,   grain = "auto",   period_from = NULL,   period_to = NULL,   fill_gaps = FALSE )

title: Coerce experience data to a Calendar object — as_calendar
text: df data.frame containing experience data per-period loss premium columns plus calendar Date column (input internal Date coercion accepts: Date, POSIXt, integer yyyy / yyyymm / yyyymmdd, ISO string). groups Column(s) used grouping (e.g., product, gender). calendar single column defining calendar-like period axis (raw name, e.g., "cy_m"). May also underwriting axis ("uy_m" etc.) single underwriting-period axis summarised time series rather development structure. loss Single character; per-period loss column df (raw name, e.g., "loss_incr"). premium Single character; per-period premium column df (raw name, e.g., "premium_incr"). Premium measure used denominator loss ratio calculations. long-term health insurance applications, risk premium commonly used. grain One "auto" (default), "M", "Q", "H", "Y". "auto" infers grain calendar value spacing. Explicit values must least coarse input grain; input binned (floored) grain aggregation. period_from Optional lower bound calendar. rows calendar >= period_from kept. period_to Optional upper bound calendar. rows calendar <= period_to kept. fill_gaps Logical; TRUE, zero-fill missing (groups, calendar) cells every group consecutive calendar sequence resolved grain. Default FALSE, raises error gaps detected.

title: Coerce experience data to a Calendar object — as_calendar
text: data.frame class "Calendar", containing following derived columns: dev Calendar index within group, defined sequential order calendar sorting ascending order. represents progression calendar periods group (e.g., 1 = first observed period, 2 = second, ...), can used align groups different starting periods common index scale. loss, loss_incr Cumulative per-period loss premium, premium_incr Cumulative per-period premium lr, lr_incr Cumulative per-period loss ratio margin, margin_incr Cumulative per-period margin profit, profit_incr Profit indicator loss_share, loss_incr_share, premium_share, premium_incr_share Proportions within calendar cell returned object also attribute "longer" containing melted long-format version (class = "CalendarLonger").

title: Coerce experience data to a Calendar object — as_calendar
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res1 <- as_calendar(   df,   groups   = "pd_cd",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" )  res2 <- as_calendar(   df,   groups      = "pd_cd",   calendar    = "cy_q",   loss        = "loss_incr",   premium     = "premium_incr",   period_from = "2023-01-01" )  head(res1) attr(res1, "longer") } # }

what: Coerce a Triangle to a Link object — as_link
title: Coerce a Triangle to a Link object — as_link
text: Derive development-link table Triangle assign Link S3 class associated summary.Link(), plot.Link(), fit_ata(), fit_intensity(), etc. methods dispatch result. Unlike as_triangle() / as_calendar() / as_total() (take raw experience data validate/aggregate ), as_link() operates Triangle (already validated upstream) reshapes link-pair rows. row corresponds one development link (cohort, ata_from -> ata_to), long-format intermediate underlying chain ladder (CL) exposure-driven (ED) workflows. Two modes produced depending exposure: Single-variable mode (exposure = NULL) age--age factor \(ata = value_{} / value_{}\), \(value\) column named target. Dual-variable mode (exposure supplied) addition loss-side ATA, exposure-driven intensity \(g = \Delta loss / premium_{}\) computed stored intensity column. Premium measure used denominator loss ratio calculations; long-term health insurance applications, risk premium commonly used.

title: Coerce a Triangle to a Link object — as_link
code: as_link(   x,   target = "loss",   exposure = NULL,   weight = NULL,   min_denom = 0,   drop_invalid = FALSE )

title: Coerce a Triangle to a Link object — as_link
text: x Triangle object. target single cumulative metric used link numerator. Must one "loss", "premium", "lr". Default "loss". Generic worker name; loss-side ATA cumulative loss column, cumulative metric Triangle may supplied. exposure Optional second cumulative metric, treated exposure anchor ED workflow. Must one "loss", "premium", "lr", must differ target. NULL (default), single-variable columns produced. weight Optional cumulative metric used WLS weight downstream summary / fit_ata calls. Must differ target. combined exposure (dual workflow anchor). min_denom Minimum denominator required compute ata intensity. target_from <= min_denom, ata becomes NA; exposure_from <= min_denom, intensity becomes NA. Default 0. drop_invalid Logical; TRUE, rows non-finite ata (single-var) non-finite intensity (dual-var) dropped. Default FALSE full link grid preserved diagnostics.

title: Coerce a Triangle to a Link object — as_link
text: data.table class "Link" columns: Always: [group], cohort, ata_from, ata_to, ata_link, target_from, target_to, target_delta, ata. exposure set: also exposure_from, exposure_to, exposure_delta, intensity. weight set: also weight. returned object carries attributes groups, cohort, dev, target, exposure (NULL), weight (NULL).

title: Coerce a Triangle to a Link object — as_link
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ tri <- as_triangle(   df,   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" )  # Single-variable: cumulative-loss link factors (ATA workflow) link_loss <- as_link(tri, target = "loss")  # Dual-variable: ED-ready link table (loss + premium) link_ed <- as_link(tri, target = "loss", exposure = "premium") head(link_ed) } # }

what: Coerce experience data to a Total object — as_total
title: Coerce experience data to a Total object — as_total
text: Validate raw experience data, aggregate single scalar row per group (collapsing cohort development axes), assign Total S3 class associated plot.Total() bar chart Total methods dispatch result. Compared as_triangle() (two-dimensional cohort × dev) as_calendar() (one-dimensional time series), as_total() zero-dimensional per group — one row portfolio aggregates. typical use high-level portfolio comparison across products, coverages, channels. function intended high-level portfolio comparison across groups products, coverages, channels. summarises: number observed cohorts (n_cohorts) first last observed periods (sales_start, sales_end) total loss total premium (cumulative) total loss ratio (lr = loss / premium) group's share total loss total premium period_from /period_to supplied, input data first restricted period window aggregation. useful comparing groups common period basis.

title: Coerce experience data to a Total object — as_total
code: as_total(   df,   groups = NULL,   cohort,   development,   loss,   premium,   period_from = NULL,   period_to = NULL,   fill_gaps = FALSE )

title: Coerce experience data to a Total object — as_total
text: df data.frame containing experience data. groups Grouping variable(s). cohort single period variable (raw name). may underwriting period ("uy_m", "uy_q", "uy_h", "uy") calendar period ("cy_m", "cy_q", "cy_h", "cy"). development single development-period variable (raw name) used count observed periods. loss Single character; per-period loss column df (raw name, e.g., "loss_incr"). premium Single character; per-period premium column df (raw name, e.g., "premium_incr"). Premium measure used denominator loss ratio calculations. long-term health insurance applications, risk premium commonly used. period_from Optional lower bound cohort. rows cohort >= period_from kept. May supplied Date, character, value coercible Date. Default NULL. period_to Optional upper bound cohort. rows cohort <= period_to kept. May supplied Date, character, value coercible Date. Default NULL. fill_gaps Logical; TRUE, zero-fill missing (groups, cohort, dev) cells aggregation every cohort consecutive dev sequence. Default FALSE. Note filling inflates n_cohorts (counts filled rows observed periods); use validate_triangle() inspect first.

title: Coerce experience data to a Total object — as_total
text: data.frame class "Total" containing: n_cohorts Number observed development periods sales_start First observed period sales_end Last observed period loss Total loss premium Total premium lr Total loss ratio (loss / premium) loss_share Share total loss premium_share Share total premium

what: Coerce experience data to a Triangle object — as_triangle
title: Coerce experience data to a Triangle object — as_triangle
text: Validate raw experience data, aggregate onto (group, cohort, dev) grid, assign Triangle S3 class downstream methods (fit_lr(), fit_loss(), backtest(), plot(), plot_triangle(), detect_maturity(), detect_regime(), detect_convergence(), ...) can dispatch result. Three steps happen inside single call: Validate — required columns present, dates coerce cleanly, grain consistent. Hard errors schema issues downstream code never receives malformed input. Standardise + aggregate — rename package-canonical column names (cohort, calendar, dev, loss, premium, ...), auto-detect grain (M / Q / H / Y) cohort spacing, derive dev (cohort, calendar), aggregate (group, cohort, dev), enrich cumulative / share / LR columns. Tag — set S3 class c("Triangle", "data.table", "data.frame") every *.Triangle method becomes available. lossratio's Triangle data.table long format (one row per (group, cohort, dev) cell) enriched columns described . name Triangle refers conceptual cohort × dev triangular region — older cohorts observed dev cells newer ones — matrix layout. auto-grain detection (grain = "auto", default) reads cohort value spacing; explicit values must least coarse input grain. user pre-bin data supply dev_* column. result contains: cumulative loss cumulative premium, per-period cumulative proportions, per-period cumulative margin, profit indicators, per-period loss ratio (lr_incr = loss_incr / premium_incr) cumulative loss ratio (lr = loss / premium). cumulative loss ratio defined : $$lr = loss / premium$$ long-term health insurance applications, risk premium commonly used premium measure. Proportion variables computed within (cohort, dev) cell: loss_incr_share    = loss_incr    / sum(loss_incr) premium_incr_share = premium_incr / sum(premium_incr) loss_share         = loss         / sum(loss) premium_share      = premium      / sum(premium) Therefore, fixed (cohort, dev) cell, proportions sum 1 across groups. useful examining composition development cell across products grouping variables.

title: Coerce experience data to a Triangle object — as_triangle
code: as_triangle(   df,   groups = NULL,   cohort,   calendar = NULL,   development = NULL,   loss,   premium,   grain = "auto",   cell_type = c("incremental", "cumulative"),   fill_gaps = FALSE )

title: Coerce experience data to a Triangle object — as_triangle
text: df data.frame containing experience data per-period loss premium columns plus cohort calendar Date columns (input internal Date coercion accepts: Date, POSIXt, integer yyyy / yyyymm / yyyymmdd, ISO string). groups Column(s) used grouping (e.g., product, gender). cohort Single column (raw name) defining underwriting / exposure period start (e.g., "uy_m"). calendar Single column (raw name) defining calendar period observation (e.g., "cy_m"). Optional – supply either calendar development (). calendar given, dev derived internally via count_periods(cohort, calendar, grain). development Single column (raw name) holding pre-computed development periods (e.g., "dev_m"). Optional – supply either calendar development (). development given, calendar axis omitted attribute (downstream calendar-diagonal logic uses cohort + dev). given, development cross-checked count_periods(cohort, calendar, grain). loss Single character; per-period loss column df (raw name, e.g., "loss_incr"). premium Single character; per-period premium column df (raw name, e.g., "premium_incr"). Premium measure used denominator loss ratio calculations. long-term health insurance applications, risk premium commonly used. grain One "auto" (default), "M", "Q", "H", "Y". "auto" infers grain cohort value spacing. Explicit values must least coarse input grain; input binned (floored) grain aggregation. cell_type One "incremental" (default) "cumulative". Whether loss premium df already hold per-period (incremental) values cumulative-within-cohort values. internal triangle always built incremental representation; "cumulative" inputs differenced first. fill_gaps Logical; TRUE, zero-fill missing (groups, cohort, dev) cells every cohort consecutive dev sequence. Default FALSE, raises error gaps detected. Use validate_triangle() inspect gaps deciding.

title: Coerce experience data to a Triangle object — as_triangle
text: data.frame class "Triangle", containing following derived columns: n_cohorts Number distinct cohorts observed loss, loss_incr Cumulative per-period loss premium, premium_incr Cumulative per-period premium lr, lr_incr Cumulative per-period loss ratio margin, margin_incr Cumulative per-period margin (premium - loss) profit, profit_incr Profit indicator (factor "pos" / "neg") loss_share, loss_incr_share Cumulative per-period proportions loss within (cohort, dev) cell premium_share, premium_incr_share Cumulative per-period proportions premium within (cohort, dev) cell Attributes set returned object: groups, cohort, calendar, grain, dev (= "dev_<lower(grain)>", e.g. "dev_m"), loss, premium, longer.

title: Coerce experience data to a Triangle object — as_triangle
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- data.frame(   pd_cd        = rep(c("P001", "P002"), each = 6),   pd_nm        = rep(c("cancer", "health"), each = 6),   uy_m         = rep(as.Date(c("2023-01-01", "2023-02-01", "2023-03-01")), 4),   cy_m         = rep(as.Date(c("2023-01-01", "2023-02-01")), 6),   loss_incr    = runif(12, 80, 120),   premium_incr = runif(12, 90, 110) )  # auto-detected monthly grain res_m <- as_triangle(   df,   groups   = "pd_cd",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" )  # explicit quarterly view (re-bins monthly input to quarterly) res_q <- as_triangle(   df,   groups   = "pd_cd",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr",   grain    = "Q" )  head(res_m) attr(res_m, "longer") } # }

what: Backtest a loss / premium / loss-ratio projection on existing data — backtest
title: Backtest a loss / premium / loss-ratio projection on existing data — backtest
text: Hold latest holdout calendar diagonals input Triangle, refit target-specific projection earlier portion, project held-cells, compare projection actual values withheld. target selected target: target = "lr" – score loss-ratio projection fit_lr(). target = "loss" – score loss projection fit_loss(). target = "premium" – score premium projection fit_premium(). /E Error (ae_err) follows standard actuarial /E convention computed cell-wise $$ae\_err = \frac{value_{actual}}{value_{proj}} - 1$$ positive values flag -projection (model -estimated; actual exceeded expected) negative values flag -projection. Aggregated development period (col_summary) calendar diagonal (diag_summary).

title: Backtest a loss / premium / loss-ratio projection on existing data — backtest
code: backtest(   x,   holdout = 6L,   target = c("lr", "loss", "premium"),   loss_method = c("sa", "ed", "cl"),   premium_method = c("cl", "ed"),   loss_alpha = 1,   premium_alpha = 1,   sigma_method = c("locf", "min_last2", "loglinear"),   recent = NULL,   loss_regime = NULL,   premium_regime = NULL,   maturity = "auto",   se_method = c("fixed", "delta"),   rho = 0.95,   conf_level = 0.95,   bootstrap = FALSE,   B = 1000,   seed = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'Backtest' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Backtest' summary(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.Backtest' print(x, ...)

title: Backtest a loss / premium / loss-ratio projection on existing data — backtest
text: x "Triangle" object ("Backtest" object S3 print() method). holdout Integer. Number latest calendar diagonals mask refitting. Default 6L. target Character scalar. projection backtest. One "lr" (default), "loss", "premium". Determines fitter called masked triangle column x treated held-actual. loss_method Method loss-side projection. Passed fit_lr() / fit_loss() method argument. One "sa", "ed", "cl". Unused target = "premium". premium_method Method premium-side projection. Passed fit_lr() / fit_loss() / fit_premium(). One "cl", "ed". loss_alpha, premium_alpha Mack alpha loss-side / premium-side chain-ladder estimation. sigma_method Tail sigma extrapolation method. Forwarded underlying fitter. recent Calendar-diagonal recency filter forwarded fitter. loss_regime, premium_regime Regime spec loss / premium side. accepts one four input types, dispatched .resolve_regime(): NULL (default) – regime filter. Regime object (e.g. detect_regime()) – used -. string "auto" – runs detect_regime() masked triangle (leakage-safe; uses data available simulated backtest cutoff). function function(tri) -> Regime – called masked triangle leakage-safe reason. premium_regime resolved independently loss_regime. maturity Maturity input. Used target = "lr" target = "loss" (stage-adaptive). Accepts one four input types, dispatched .resolve_maturity(): NULL – skip maturity filtering. Maturity object (e.g. detect_maturity() maturity_at()) – used -. Caller takes responsibility leakage pre-computation. string "auto" (default) – runs detect_maturity() masked triangle (last holdout calendar diagonals removed), avoiding look-ahead leakage. function function(tri) -> Maturity (e.g. maturity_spec()) – called masked triangle leakage-safe reason. se_method Standard-error composition fit_lr(). Unused target = "loss" / target = "premium". rho Loss-premium correlation used fit_lr() delta method. Unused target = "loss" / target = "premium". conf_level Confidence level fit_lr() / fit_loss() intervals. Unused target = "premium". bootstrap, B, seed Bootstrap controls fit_lr(). Unused target = "loss" / target = "premium". ... Additional arguments passed underlying fitter. object "Backtest" object. Used S3 summary() method.

what: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
title: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
text: Identify first dev \(k^{**}\) projected portfolio loss ratio observed stable maximum available development period \(V\). Three complementary stability criteria computed LR backtest path; user selects one defines \(k^{**}\) via method =. Notation mapping (code <-> math): Standard chain-ladder convention: \(\) indexes cohort (origin period), \(k\) indexes development period. maturity point \(k^*\) convergence point \(k^{**}\) live \(k\) axis. Earlier paper drafts used \(v\) (valuation) index Section 11; unify \(k\) consistency. Stability methods (sequence drives pass): "window" Local stability: drift_window[] < max_drift. Fast, misses slow monotone drift fits max_drift per step. "tail" (default, reserving-safe) Global stability: drift_tail[] < max_drift. Catches monotone drift. first passing dev later (conservative) "window". "slope" Trend test: |slope[]| < max_slope. Explicit -trend check; sensitive non-linear trajectories. "" Strictest: three pass simultaneously. four pass vectors (pass_window, pass_tail, pass_slope, pass) underlying diagnostic series returned regardless chosen method, analyst can inspect every criterion re-decide. Across methods, cross-cohort agreement clause dispersion[] < max_dispersion required addition. replaces earlier formulation \(R_k < c \cdot \hat{SE}^{param}_k\) (paper Section 11). paper's SE-normalised form asymptotically broken large portfolios: \(\hat{SE}^{param}\) shrinks \(1/\sqrt{n}\) \(R_k\) structural noise floor, ratio diverges criterion never fires. Caveat (reserving): detected conv_k reflects stability dev_max (\(K_{\max}\)) – asymptotic guarantee projection drift past \(K_{\max}\). Treat conv_k diagnostic ", observe stable", guarantee future stability. reserving applications, prefer method = "tail" "" "window", attach IBNR margin via fit_lr$summary SE/CI columns, weigh evidence span (dev_max - conv_k): conv_k near dev_max thin evidence.

title: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
code: detect_convergence(   triangle,   method = c("tail", "window", "slope", "all"),   max_drift = 0.01,   max_slope = 0.001,   max_dispersion = 0.15,   window = 5L,   mat_k = NULL,   holdout_max = NULL,   min_n_cohorts = 5L,   ... )

title: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
text: triangle Triangle object (typically as_triangle()). method stability criterion defines conv_k. One "tail" (default), "window", "slope", "". See description semantics reserving caveat. max_drift Upper bound drift metric (window tail), LR units. Default 0.01 (1pp). Raise noisier longer-tail books. max_slope Upper bound |slope[]|, OLS slope \(LR \sim k\) \([k, K_{\max}]\), LR-per-dev units. Default 1e-3 (0.1pp per dev). Used method = "slope" / "". max_dispersion Upper bound cross-cohort dispersion \(\hat{D}_k\). Default 0.15. window Drift window length \(W\) (dev steps): number consecutive valuations used "window" method compute drift_window. Default 5L. Note: functions package also expose window argument (e.g. detect_regime() e-divisive segment width); controls drift metric, e-divisive algorithm. mat_k Pre-computed maturity point. NULL, computed via detect_maturity() applied lr-based ATA. holdout_max Maximum holdout depth used rolling backtest. NULL, set max(window, floor((dev_max - mat_k) / 2)). min_n_cohorts Minimum number cohorts required compute \(\hat{D}_v\). Default 5L. ... Additional arguments forwarded backtest() (thence fit_lr()), e.g. loss_method, recent, loss_regime.

title: Find the development period at which the loss ratio estimate stabilises — detect_convergence
text: object class Convergence (named list). Includes slots tabulated notation mapping (dev_max, dev_cand, lr, revision, drift_window, drift_tail, slope, dispersion), per-method pass vectors (pass_window, pass_tail, pass_slope, pass), threshold parameters, metadata attributes (groups, target, dispatcher).

what: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
title: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
text: Identify first mature age--age (ata) link Triangle. Internally builds single-variable Link table, computes per-link diagnostic via summary.Link() model = "ata", locates first link whose statistics satisfy maturity criteria. Maturity determined using combination : cv < max_cv rse < max_rse valid_ratio >= min_valid_ratio n_valid >= min_n_valid optional consecutive maturity min_run ata links cv rse must satisfied simultaneously. cv captures raw variability observed ata factors across cohorts, rse reflects precision WLS-estimated factor. Using criteria together provides robust maturity assessment either alone. Default target = "loss" (cumulative loss). Maturity chain ladder methodologically property loss development: ATA factors cumulative loss stabilize chain ladder becomes reliable, turn makes downstream LR projection reliable. ATA factors lr (ratio two cumulative quantities) carry additional noise tend give less precise maturity decisions. Override target specifically want maturity premium development another cumulative metric.

title: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
code: detect_maturity(   x,   target = "loss",   groups = NULL,   weight = NULL,   alpha = 1,   max_cv = 0.15,   max_rse = 0.05,   min_valid_ratio = 0.5,   min_n_valid = 3L,   min_run = 2L )

title: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
text: x Triangle object. target Cumulative metric link factor. Default "loss" (chain-ladder convention; see Description). Forwarded as_link(). groups Optional character subset attr(x, "groups") selecting columns define maturity partition. Maturity typically structural property development curve driven coverage rather demographic mix (age, channel, ...), Triangle aggregated c("coverage", "age_band", "channel") may still want per-coverage maturity. NULL (default) keeps current Triangle grouping (fully backward compatible). character(0) pools across groups returns single global maturity row. non-NULL, non-empty value must subset attr(x, "groups"); column order irrelevant. requested groups coarser Triangle grouping, underlying loss / premium / lr columns re-aggregated coarser partition computing ata links. weight Optional WLS weight variable. Forwarded as_link(). alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure underlying WLS fit. Default 1. Forwarded summary.Link(). max_cv Maximum allowed coefficient variation. Default 0.15. max_rse Maximum allowed relative standard error. Default 0.05. min_valid_ratio Minimum proportion finite ata values required. Default 0.5. min_n_valid Minimum number finite ata factors required. Default 3L. min_run Minimum number consecutive ata links satisfying maturity criteria. Default 2L.

title: Find ata maturity by group — detect_maturity
text: data.table class "Maturity" containing one row per group. Columns include ata_from, change (maturity point, .e. -index first mature ata link), ata_link, diagnostic statistics (mean, median, wt, cv, f, f_se, rse, sigma, n_cohorts, n_valid, n_inf, n_nan, valid_ratio). mature link found, values group NA.

what: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
text: Detect structural change points sequence cohort-level development trajectories. underwriting cohort (indexed cohort "Triangle" object) treated feature vector whose entries selected target metric observed development periods 1, ..., window. Cohorts ordered underwriting period tested structural shifts multivariate sequence. Multi-group Triangle inputs supported: detection runs independently per group, results combined single Regime object whose $changes, $labels, etc. carry group column. Single-group input retains original scalar / Date-vector / matrix layout backward compatibility. Three detection strategies supported: "e_divisive" Multivariate non-parametric divisive change-point detection via ecp::e.divisive(). number regimes determined data; significant changes sig_level retained. Preferred number regimes known advance. "pelt" Univariate mean change-point detection via changepoint::cpt.mean() PELT algorithm applied first principal component cohort feature matrix. Fast may return multiple changes. "hclust" Ward hierarchical clustering scaled cohort feature matrix, cut n_regimes clusters. Ignores time ordering – useful sanity check since non-adjacent cohorts may cluster together trajectory pattern strictly chronological.

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
code: detect_regime(   x,   target = "lr",   by = NULL,   window = "auto",   method = c("e_divisive", "pelt", "hclust"),   n_regimes = NULL,   sig_level = 0.05,   min_size = 3L,   treatment = c("latest_only", "segment_wise"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'Regime' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Regime' summary(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'summary.Regime' print(x, ...)

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
text: x object class "Triangle". May contain one groups (per-group detection runs independently). Also used S3 print() method Regime objects. target Trajectory variable. Default "lr" (cumulative loss ratio). Accepts column Triangle (e.g. "lr", "loss", "premium", "loss_incr", "premium_incr"), plus three diagnostic derived targets computed inline per (group, cohort): "loss_ata" Loss age--age factor loss[k+1] / loss[k] — multiplicative loss development speed (CL $f_k$). "premium_ata" Premium age--age factor — form premium. "loss_ed" Loss intensity (loss[k] - loss[k-1]) / premium[k-1] — additive, exposure-anchored (ED model's $g_k$). "premium_ed" Alias "premium_ata" — two differ constant (premium_ata - 1), PCA standardization detection removes shift, yield identical regime changes. Provided API symmetry loss_ata / loss_ed pair. Derived targets drop first dev row per cohort (predecessor), re-index dev detection sees contiguous sequence. See vignette("regime") "Choice target" section guidance target matches suspected event. Grouping column(s) per-combination detection. NULL (default) reuses Triangle's attr(x, "groups") non-empty — detect_regime(tri) dispatches per group automatically — otherwise falls back pooled detection. Pass = character(0) force pooled detection multi-group Triangle, character vector (subset names(x)) dispatch explicit combo, e.g. = "coverage" = c("channel", "coverage"). window Trajectory window. Integer (e.g., 12L) fixed window, string "auto" (default) — resolves group's maturity via detect_maturity(), falling back 6L maturity unavailable (NA, pooled mode, mismatching Triangle's attr("groups")). Cohorts fewer resolved window observed periods dropped. method One "e_divisive", "pelt", "hclust". n_regimes Integer. Number regimes force. NULL means auto-detect "e_divisive" "pelt"; ignored (required equal requested value) "hclust", default 2. sig_level Significance level "e_divisive". Default 0.05. min_size Minimum segment size "e_divisive". Default 3. treatment downstream fits apply Regime $changes contains multiple change points. One : "latest_only" (default) Collapse recent change date drop pre-latest-change cohorts. Single pooled factor estimate surviving (post-latest-change) cohorts. "segment_wise" Preserve change points. segment (consecutive cohorts adjacent changes) gets factor estimate, cohort projected using segment's factor. Recommended multi-regime + long-tail data opt "latest_only" lose self-regime responsiveness older cohorts. ... Reserved future use. object object class "Regime". Used S3 summary() method.

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
text: object class "Regime". single-group input: call Matched call. method Detection method used. target Trajectory variable used detection. window Trajectory window per combo. Scalar integer single combo analysed; integer vector (one per surviving combo, order $labels / $changes group rows) otherwise. window_mode Either "auto" (resolved per group via detect_maturity()) "manual" (user-supplied integer). cohort Period variable x. labels data.table one row per analysed cohort: [..., cohort, regime, regime_id]. Group columns prepended resolves non-empty vector. changes data.table detected regime changes columns [..., change, regime_id, pre_value, post_value, magnitude]. regime_id = id regime STARTS change (pre-change regime regime_id - 1); matches $labels$regime_id. pre_value / post_value mean target cohort × dev trajectory windows pre- / post-change regimes; magnitude = |post_value - pre_value|. Empty (zero rows) change detected. n_regimes Number regimes detected. Scalar integer single-combo detection; named integer vector (keyed combo) multi-combo. trajectory Cohort × dev feature matrix used detection. Single matrix single combo; named list matrices multi-combo. pca prcomp object (single combo) named list prcomp objects (multi-combo). dropped Cohorts excluded due window window constraint. Vector (single) / named list (multi). multi_group Logical flag; TRUE detection ran multiple group combos. treatment Either "latest_only" "segment_wise" — value supplied via treatment argument. Read downstream fits (fit_ata(), fit_intensity(), fit_cl(), fit_ed()) decide whether collapse latest change (drop pre-change cohorts, single pooled factor) estimate per-segment factors.

title: Detect structural regime shifts across underwriting cohorts — detect_regime
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(experience) tri_sur <- as_triangle(   experience[coverage == "SUR"],   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" )  # Hierarchical clustering (no extra package dependency) r <- detect_regime(tri_sur, method = "hclust",                           n_regimes = 2L) print(r) summary(r) plot(r)  # ecp divisive change-point detection (requires the ecp package) r_ecp <- detect_regime(tri_sur, method = "e_divisive")  # Multi-group: detection per coverage tri_all <- as_triangle(   experience,   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" ) r_all <- detect_regime(tri_all, by = "coverage", method = "e_divisive") print(r_all$changes) } # }

NULL missing, dt returned unchanged. grp Character vector group columns (may empty). coh Single column name cohort variable (e.g. cohort). dev Single column name development variable (e.g. dev Triangle objects, ata_from ATA/ED objects). dev_split Optional SA-boundary specifier. Accepts: NULL – SA boundary; recent wedge applies every row. single non-NA numeric scalar – maturity target dev (= ata_to, first CL-region dev). recent filter applied rows dev >= dev_split (CL region); rows dev < dev_split (ED region) kept unconditionally. data.table [grp..., dev_split] – per-group SA boundary (different k* per group). group columns must subset grp. row dt looks dev_split via left-join; rows whose group matching entry (NA join) treated dev_split = NULL row (recent wedge applies dev ).

what: Pick the divisor that produces the shortest formatted median label — .auto_divisor
title: Pick the divisor that produces the shortest formatted median label — .auto_divisor
text: Internal helper used amount_divisor = "auto". Considers SI/financial prefix set {1, 1e3, 1e6, 1e9, 1e12} (unit / thousand / million / billion / trillion) picks divisor minimises nchar(sprintf("%.1f", median / divisor)). Candidates whose median label rounds exactly "0.0" disqualified (precision loss). ties, smallest divisor wins significant digit survives (e.g. "6.7" "0.7"). Falls back 1 data finite positive values. candidate set deliberately uses powers 1000 (1e7 / 1e8): rule's "tie-break smallest divisor" already keeps label 1.0 999.X, finer grid change compactness. trade-accepts tails wide distribution may round label – heatmap colour fill carries precise value; -cell label numeric hint. Examples: median 6.6e7  -> divisor 1e6, label "66.6" (4 chars; /1e9 give "0.1", rounds away signal 1e6 wins smallest-divisor tie-break). median 5e5    -> divisor 1e3, label "500.0" (5 chars). median 5e9    -> divisor 1e9, label "5.0" (3 chars).

title: Loss ratio SE: lr_se = SE(L/P) — .compute_lr_se
text: LR-specific internal helper. Two variants: "fixed" (default) Premium treated fixed (non-random). \(\mathrm{SE}(L/P) = \mathrm{SE}(L) / P\). Strictly degenerate case delta method Var(P) = 0 Cov(L,P) = 0. "delta" First-order Taylor (delta method) including premium uncertainty loss-premium correlation rho: \(\mathrm{Var}(L/P) \approx (\mathrm{SE}(L)/P)^2 +       (L \cdot \mathrm{SE}(P) / P^2)^2 -       2 \rho L \mathrm{SE}(L) \mathrm{SE}(P) / P^3\). variance clipped zero square root (high rho can drive linearised estimate negative). exported; called fit_lr(). "fixed" branch encodes actuarial assumption earned premium known (estimated), helper generic ratio-SE utility.

title: Loss ratio SE: lr_se = SE(L/P) — .compute_lr_se
code: .compute_lr_se(   loss,   premium,   se_loss,   se_premium = NULL,   method = c("fixed", "delta"),   rho = 0.95 )

title: Loss ratio SE: lr_se = SE(L/P) — .compute_lr_se
text: loss Ultimate loss vector (L). premium Ultimate premium vector (E). se_loss SE(L). se_premium SE(P). Unused "fixed"; may NULL. method One "fixed" (default) "delta". rho Loss-premium correlation (-1, 1). Used "delta". Default 0.95.

title: Loss ratio SE: lr_se = SE(L/P) — .compute_lr_se
text: numeric vector length loss.

what: Replace CL multiplicative SE with ED additive SE on a CLFit's $full — .ed_replace_se
title: Replace CL multiplicative SE with ED additive SE on a CLFit's $full — .ed_replace_se
text: Point projection (target_proj) preserved – identical CL self-weighted ED. variance accumulation differs: CL recursion: proc_{k+1} = f^2 * proc_k + sigma^2 * C_k (multiplicative scaling – prior variance amplified f^2 step). ED recursion: proc_{k+1} = proc_k + sigma^2 * C_k (additive – prior variance carried forward unchanged). share per-link sigma^2 f_var (= Var(f_hat_k)) estimates. recursion per (group, cohort).

title: Replace CL multiplicative SE with ED additive SE on a CLFit's $full — .ed_replace_se

what: Estimate ED intensity via weighted least squares — .lm_ed
title: Estimate ED intensity via weighted least squares — .lm_ed
text: Internal helper fits one -intercept weighted linear model per development link: $$\Delta C^L_{,k+1} = g_k \cdot C^P_{,k} + \varepsilon_{,k}$$ Weights proportional \(1 / (C^P_{,k})^{2 - \alpha}\), corresponding variance assumption \(\mathrm{Var}(\Delta C^L_{,k+1}) \propto (C^P_{,k})^{\alpha}\).

title: Estimate ED intensity via weighted least squares — .lm_ed
code: .lm_ed(x, alpha = 1, na_rm = TRUE, tol = 1e-12)

what: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_link
title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_link
text: Internal helper fits one -intercept weighted linear model per age--age link: $$C_{,k+1} = f_k \cdot C_{,k} + \varepsilon_{,k}$$ Weights proportional \(w_{,k} / C_{,k}^{2 - \alpha}\), \(w_{,k}\) either constant column supplied via weights. corresponds Mack's variance assumption \(\mathrm{Var}(C_{,k+1} \mid C_{,k}) \propto C_{,k}^{\alpha}\). one observation available link, factor computed directly target_to / target_from standard errors set NA. Near-zero values f_se sigma (tol) set zero avoid numerical noise essentially perfect fits.

title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_link
code: .lm_link(x, weights = 1, alpha = 1, na_rm = TRUE, tol = 1e-12)

title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_link
text: x object class "Link". weights Either length-one numeric scalar (default 1) single column name present Link data provides per-row weights. alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure. Default 1. na_rm Logical; TRUE (default), rows non-finite non-positive target_from dropped fitting. Note target_to = 0 permitted, zero cumulative values valid observations (e.g. claims yet developed early development periods). tol Non-negative numeric scalar. Values tol set zero. Default 1e-12.

title: Estimate age-to-age factors via weighted least squares — .lm_link
