what: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: Compute group-wise summary statistics age--age factors object class "Link". helper backs summary.Link() dispatcher model = "ata". serves two purposes: Diagnostics: provides descriptive statistics (mean, median, wt, cv) help user assess stability consistency observed ata factors across cohorts. Estimation: fits -intercept weighted least squares model per ata link produce WLS-estimated factor (f), standard error (f_se), relative standard error (rse), Mack sigma (sigma). used downstream detect_maturity() fit_ata().

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
code: .summarize_link_ata(object, alpha = 1, digits = 3, ...)

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: object object class "Link", typically produced as_link(). alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure WLS fit. Default 1. digits Number decimal places round numeric columns. Default 3. Pass NULL skip rounding. ... Additional arguments passed internal WLS estimation.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: data.table class "ATASummary" containing one row per ata link: ata_from, ata_to, ata_link Link identifiers. mean Arithmetic mean observed ata factors. median Median observed ata factors. wt Volume-weighted mean: \(\sum C_{,k+1} / \sum C_{,k}\), independent alpha. cv Coefficient variation observed ata factors (\(SD / mean\)). Used detect_maturity() assess stability. f WLS-estimated factor. Equals wt alpha = 2 zero target_from rows present. f_se Standard error WLS-estimated factor. rse Relative standard error WLS-estimated factor (\(f\_se / f\)). sigma Mack sigma (residual standard deviation WLS fit). Used Mack variance estimation. n_cohorts Total number observations link. n_valid Number finite ata values. n_inf Number infinite ata values. n_nan Number NaN ata values. valid_ratio Proportion finite ata values (\(n\_valid / n\_cohorts\)).

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: wt f weighted averages observed ata factors, differ weights assigned observations included: wt Volume-weighted mean: \(wt = \sum C_{,k+1} / \sum C_{,k}\). Computed rows target_from target_to finite, including rows either value zero. Independent alpha. f WLS-estimated factor. rows target_from > 0 used, since target_from = 0 causes numerical issues WLS weights (\(w = value\_from^{\alpha}\)). alpha = 2, f wt numerically equivalent (assuming zero target_from rows). alpha \ne 2, diverge. Therefore wt f can differ two reasons: Zero exclusion: rows target_from = 0 included wt excluded f. typically affects early development periods cohorts yet accumulated claims. Alpha effect: alpha \ne 2, WLS weights differ volume weights used wt, leading different estimates. Comparing wt f can help diagnose whether choice alpha materially affects estimated factor.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: input "Link" object contains weight column (added as_link() weight supplied), column automatically used WLS weight place target_from. useful target = "lr", target_from carries exposure information external exposure variable premium used instead.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: coefficient variation defined : $$cv = \frac{SD(f_k)}{\bar{f}_k}$$ \(f_k\) individual observed ata values link \(k\) \(\bar{f}_k\) arithmetic mean. cv reflects relative spread observed factors across cohorts, regardless exposure scale. used detect_maturity() one criteria determining maturity point.

title: Internal: summarise age-to-age factor statistics from a Link table — .summarize_link_ata
text: relative standard error defined : $$rse = \frac{SE(\hat{f}_k)}{\hat{f}_k}$$ \(SE(\hat{f}_k)\) standard error WLS-estimated factor. Unlike cv, treats cohorts equally, rse gives weight cohorts larger exposures (via WLS weights). small rse indicates WLS estimate precise, tends occur : (1) many cohorts, (2) exposures large, (3) observed ata values consistent across cohorts.

title: Summarise ED intensity statistics — .summarize_link_ed
text: Internal helper computes group-wise summary statistics incremental loss intensity \(g\) dual-variable Link object (built exposure set). Dispatched via summary.Link() model = "ed". Two purposes: Diagnostics: provides descriptive statistics (mean, median, wt, cv) help user assess stability consistency observed \(g\) values across cohorts. Estimation: fits -intercept weighted least squares model per development link produce WLS-estimated intensity (g), standard error (g_se), relative standard error (rse), residual sigma (sigma). used downstream fit_ed().

title: Summarise ED intensity statistics — .summarize_link_ed
code: .summarize_link_ed(object, alpha = 1, digits = 5, ...)

title: Summarise ED intensity statistics — .summarize_link_ed

title: Summarise ED intensity statistics — .summarize_link_ed

Independent alpha. g WLS-estimated intensity lm(target_delta ~ exposure_from + 0). rows exposure_from > 0 used. alpha = 2, g wt numerically equivalent.

title: Unified theme dispatcher (internal) — .switch_theme

"save": embedding spreadsheet tools Excel; fixed axis / title sizes, plot background untouched. "shiny": embedding Shiny apps; "save" plus configurable transparent plot background. Setting *.size argument 0 replaces element ggplot2::element_blank().

text: Unlike ATA factors, CV / RSE drive detect_maturity() threshold, ED intensities behave differently — \(g_k \0\) late development CV / RSE blow construction, instability. fit_intensity() therefore deliberately omits maturity parameter, detect_maturity() rejects IntensityFit input informative error.

what: Fit a loss projection on a Triangle — fit_loss
title: Fit a loss projection on a Triangle — fit_loss
text: Project cumulative loss across cohort x development grid. Three methods supported via method: "sa" (default) Stage-adaptive. Exposure-driven (ED) maturity point, chain ladder (CL) . "ed" Pure exposure-driven (additive) across dev periods. "cl" Pure Mack chain ladder (multiplicative). function loss-side counterpart fit_premium() role-specific dispatcher layer (see ARCHITECTURE.md). owns loss projection – premium projection delegated fit_premium() (called internally premium_fit = NULL), loss-ratio composition delta method handled fit_lr().

title: Fit a loss projection on a Triangle — fit_loss
text: x "Triangle" object. standardized "loss" "premium" columns used (as_triangle() produces ). method One "sa" (default), "ed", "cl". alpha Variance-structure exponent loss fit. Default 1. regime Optional regime specification applied loss-side premium-side estimation. Accepts four input types: NULL (default) regime filter. Regime object Use -. Typically built via detect_regime() regime_at(). "auto" Detect regime internally via detect_regime(x) input triangle. Function / closure user-supplied function taking triangle returning Regime object (NULL). Behavior depends method: SA uses hybrid 2-pass filter (cohort cut ED phase, calendar-diagonal wedge CL phase); ED/CL use simple cohort cut. resolved Regime applied internal fit_premium() call – callers needing asymmetric loss/premium split use fit_lr() instead. premium_fit Optional pre-built PremiumFit (fit_premium()) supplying premium projection. NULL, fit_loss() calls fit_premium() internally using premium_method, premium_alpha, resolved regime. premium_method One "cl" (default) "ed". Used premium_fit = NULL. default matches historical fit_lr() premium choice. premium_alpha Variance-structure exponent premium fit. Default 1. sigma_method Sigma extrapolation. One "locf" (default), "min_last2", "loglinear". recent Optional positive integer; calendar-diagonal filter. maturity Optional maturity specification. Accepts four input types: NULL maturity filter. SA mode requires maturity, disables ED / CL modes. Maturity object Use -. Typically built via detect_maturity() maturity_at(). "auto" (default) Detect maturity internally via detect_maturity(x) input triangle. Function / closure user-supplied function taking triangle returning Maturity object (e.g. maturity_spec()) deferred custom-config detection. conf_level Confidence level analytical CI loss projection (loss_ci_lower, loss_ci_upper). Default 0.95.

what: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_lr
title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_lr
text: Unified interface loss ratio projection "Triangle" object. Three projection methods available: "sa" (default) Uses exposure-driven (ED) estimation maturity chain ladder (CL) maturity. maturity: age--age factors volatile, exposure-driven projection \(\Delta C^L = g_k \cdot C^P_k\) anchors estimate premium volume. maturity: age--age factors stable, chain ladder projection \(C^L_{k+1} = f_k \cdot C^L_k\) preserves cohort's observed level. "ed" Exposure-driven development periods. future increments \(g_k \cdot C^P_k\). "cl" Chain ladder development periods. Equivalent classical Mack model. cases, exposure projected forward using chain ladder: $$\hat{C}^P_{,k+1} = f^P_k \cdot \hat{C}^P_{,k}$$ function composition layer fit_loss() fit_premium(): delegates loss projection fit_loss(), retrieves embedded PremiumFit, composes loss-ratio point + variance via delta method (se_method = "fixed" "delta"). See ARCHITECTURE.md layered design.

title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_lr
code: fit_lr(   x,   method = c("sa", "ed", "cl"),   loss_alpha = 1,   loss_regime = NULL,   premium_method = c("cl", "ed"),   premium_alpha = 1,   premium_regime = NULL,   sigma_method = c("locf", "min_last2", "loglinear"),   recent = NULL,   maturity = "auto",   se_method = c("fixed", "delta"),   rho = 0.95,   conf_level = 0.95,   bootstrap = FALSE,   B = 1000,   seed = NULL )

title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_lr
text: x object class "Triangle". standardized "loss" "premium" columns used (as_triangle() produces ). method One "sa" (default), "ed", "cl". loss_alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure loss estimation. Default 1. loss_regime Optional regime specification loss-side filter. Accepts four input types: NULL (default) regime filter. Regime object Use -. Typically built via detect_regime() regime_at(). "auto" Detect regime internally via detect_regime(x) input triangle. Function / closure user-supplied function taking triangle returning Regime object (NULL). Behavior depends method: "sa" Hybrid filter. Pre-change cohorts dropped development periods maturity point (ED phase); post-maturity (CL) cells use recent-diagonal window across cohorts. preserves CL stability protecting ED intensities regime change. "ed", "cl" Simple cohort cut: cohorts strictly change date excluded estimation. premium_method One "cl" (default) "ed". Forwarded fit_premium() constructing premium projection. premium_alpha Numeric scalar premium chain ladder. Default 1. premium_regime Premium-side regime specification. four input types loss_regime (NULL / Regime / "auto" / function). Default NULL – premium fit full triangle independently loss_regime (lazy default). Set explicitly regime shift affects premium accrual . sigma_method Sigma extrapolation method. One "locf" (default), "min_last2", "loglinear". recent Optional positive integer estimation window. Default NULL. maturity Optional maturity specification. Accepts four input types: NULL maturity filter. Disables SA-mode switch detection. Maturity object Use -. Typically built via detect_maturity() maturity_at(). "auto" (default) Detect maturity internally via detect_maturity(x) input triangle. Function / closure user-supplied function taking triangle returning Maturity object (e.g. maturity_spec()) deferred custom-config detection. method = "sa", also determines switch point ED CL phases. se_method Method computing lr_se = SE(L/P). One : "fixed" (default) Premium treated fixed (non-random). \(\mathrm{SE}(L/P) = \mathrm{SE}(L) / P\). Strictly, delta method Var(P) = 0 Cov(L,P) = 0, .e., degenerate case assumption premium known. "delta" Full delta method including premium uncertainty loss-premium correlation rho: $$\mathrm{Var}(L/P) \approx \frac{\mathrm{Var}(L)}{P^2}         + \frac{L^2 \mathrm{Var}(P)}{P^4}         - \frac{2 \rho L \mathrm{SE}(L) \mathrm{SE}(P)}{P^3}$$ rho Numeric scalar (-1, 1); assumed correlation ultimate loss ultimate premium. used se_method = "delta". Default 0.95, matching strong positive correlation typically observed cumulative loss cumulative premium long-tail health portfolios (analogous paid/incurred correlation used Munich chain ladder). conf_level Confidence level used lr_ci_lower/lr_ci_upper cohort summary. Default 0.95. bootstrap Logical; TRUE, parameter process variance derived via residual bootstrap rather analytical delta method. Default FALSE. B Integer number bootstrap replications. Used bootstrap = TRUE. Default 1000. seed Optional integer seed reproducible bootstrap. Default NULL.

title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_lr
text: object class "LRFit".

title: Fit loss ratio projection model — fit_lr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(experience) tri <- as_triangle(   experience[coverage == "SUR"],   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" )  # Stage-adaptive (default): ED before maturity, CL after lr_sa <- fit_lr(tri, method = "sa") summary(lr_sa) plot(lr_sa)  # Pure exposure-driven for all development periods lr_ed <- fit_lr(tri, method = "ed")  # Pure chain ladder (Mack-style) for all development periods lr_cl <- fit_lr(tri, method = "cl") } # }

what: Fit a chain ladder projection on the premium (exposure) triangle — fit_premium
title: Fit a chain ladder projection on the premium (exposure) triangle — fit_premium
text: Project cumulative premium across cohort x development grid chain ladder estimator. Two variance recursions supported: "ed" (default) Additive recursion. Empirically robust long-projection premium triangles – multiplicative scaling classical CL recursion can blow cohort-wise heterogeneity (regime changes premium, channel changes, amendments). See dev/premium_projection.qmd. "cl" Mack (1993) multiplicative recursion. Point projection identical ED; SE accumulation differs. methods share point estimate – self-weighted ED premium mathematically equivalent chain ladder column (f_k = 1 + g_k). operational difference cumulative variance propagated forward.

title: Fit a chain ladder projection on the premium (exposure) triangle — fit_premium
code: fit_premium(   x,   method = c("ed", "cl"),   alpha = 1,   regime = NULL,   sigma_method = c("locf", "min_last2", "loglinear"),   recent = NULL,   tail = FALSE,   conf_level = 0.95 )

title: Fit a chain ladder projection on the premium (exposure) triangle — fit_premium
text: x "Triangle" object. standardized "premium" column used projection target. method One "ed" (default) "cl". alpha Numeric scalar controlling variance structure passed fit_ata(). Default 1. regime Optional regime specification (premium side). Accepts four input types: NULL (default) regime filter. Regime object Use -. Typically built via detect_regime() regime_at(). "auto" Detect regime internally via detect_regime(x, target = "lr") input triangle. Function / closure user-supplied function(tri) -> Regime deferred custom-config detection. Pre-change cohorts (cohorts resolved Regime's change date) excluded premium factor estimation. sigma_method Sigma extrapolation method. One "locf" (default), "min_last2", "loglinear". recent Optional positive integer; recent calendar-diagonal filter underlying ATA fit. Default NULL. tail Logical; whether apply tail factor. Default FALSE. conf_level Confidence level analytical CI premium projection (premium_ci_lower, premium_ci_upper). Default 0.95.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(experience) tri <- as_triangle(   experience[coverage == "SUR"],   groups   = "coverage",   cohort   = "uy_m",   calendar = "cy_m",   loss     = "loss_incr",   premium  = "premium_incr" )  # ED-additive recursion (default; robust on long projections) pf <- fit_premium(tri) summary(pf)  # CL-multiplicative recursion (Mack) pf_cl <- fit_premium(tri, method = "cl") } # }

what: lossratio: Loss Ratio Analytics for Long-Term Health Insurance — lossratio-package

Provides cohort development period, calendar period, total aggregation frameworks diagnostics, applies cumulative loss exposure setting sufficient sample per cell.

title: Construct a Maturity object from manually specified maturity points — maturity_at

what: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
text: Captures detect_maturity() arguments without running detection. Returns closure consumer (fit_* backtest()) invokes internal triangle. point conditional / deferred detection – value $k^*$ depends cells caller decides expose: fit_lr() / fit_loss(), spec invoked full triangle user passed . backtest(), spec invoked masked triangle holdout fold, never full triangle. Held-diagonals removed detect_maturity() sees data, detected $k^*$ depends cells masked fit can also see. leakage-safe contract maturity_spec(). Contrast maturity_at(), produces eager "Maturity" object whose value fixed construction time (independent fold's masked data). Use maturity_spec() want $k^*$ re-detected per fold backtest honestly answers "given data available fold, picked?" Use maturity_at() want fixed value tested across folds.

title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
code: maturity_spec(...)

title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
text: ... kwargs passed verbatim detect_maturity() spec invoked (e.g. target, groups, min_run, max_cv, max_rse, min_valid_ratio, min_n_valid).

title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
text: function one argument ("Triangle") returning "Maturity" object. caller decides triangle pass (full vs. masked); inside backtest() always masked training triangle.

title: Build a lazy maturity detection spec — maturity_spec
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Capture detection arguments, defer execution until fit time. spec <- maturity_spec(min_run = 2, max_cv = 0.04)  # In fit_lr(): closure is invoked on the user's `tri`. fit <- fit_lr(tri, maturity = maturity_spec(min_run = 2))  # In backtest(): closure is invoked on the *masked* triangle of # each holdout fold, so detected k* never peeks at held-out cells. bt <- backtest(tri, holdout = 6L,                maturity = maturity_spec(min_run = 2, max_cv = 0.04)) } # }

"detail" lr / lr, show ratio percent , next line, associated loss / premium amounts. amount proportion metrics, falls back "value". label_size Numeric label text size forwarded ggshort::ggtable(). Defaults 3 label_style = "value" 2.5 label_style = "detail" (two-line labels need smaller size fit). label appearance fields (family, color, hjust, ...) fall back ggshort defaults. amount_divisor Numeric scaling factor applied amount variables (e.g., loss, loss_incr, premium, premium_incr, margin, margin_incr) plotting. Default "auto" picks largest divisor {1, 1e3, 1e6, 1e7, 1e8, 1e9} median displayed value still least 1, minimising label digit count. nrow, ncol Number rows columns passed ggplot2::facet_wrap(). theme string passed .switch_theme() ("view", "save", "shiny"). ... Additional arguments passed .switch_theme().

what: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
text: User-facing helper hand-specifying regime change (set per-group changes) without running detect_regime(). returned "Regime" object plugs function consumes Regime — fit_lr(), fit_loss(), fit_premium(), backtest(), regime-change resolver — carrying $changes schema detect_regime() output. Argument syntax mirrors data.frame() / data.table(): named vectors equal length, one must change. named arguments treated group columns.

title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
code: regime_at(..., treatment = c("latest_only", "segment_wise"))

title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at
text: ... Named vectors equal length. Must include change (coercible Date; start--regime date post-change regime). named arguments interpreted group column values (e.g. coverage, channel). group columns result pooled (single-row) Regime. treatment downstream fits apply Regime $changes contains multiple change points. "latest_only" (default) collapses recent change drops pre-latest cohorts (single pooled factor). "segment_wise" preserves changes estimates one factor per segment (cohort projected segment's factor). See detect_regime() full semantics.

title: Construct a Regime object from manually specified regime changes — regime_at

Detection-specific slots (labels, trajectory, pca, dropped, n_regimes, window, window_mode, pca) left empty / NA object can still printed consumed clearly distinguishable detected Regime.

what: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
text: Captures detect_regime() arguments without running detection. Returns closure consumer (fit_* backtest()) invokes internal triangle. point conditional / deferred detection – change points depend cells caller decides expose: fit_lr() / fit_loss() / fit_premium(), spec invoked full triangle user passed . backtest(), spec invoked masked triangle holdout fold, never full triangle. Held-diagonals removed detect_regime() sees data, detected change points depend cells masked fit can also see. leakage-safe contract regime_spec(). Contrast regime_at(), produces eager "Regime" object whose change points fixed construction time (independent fold's masked data). Use regime_spec() want change points re-detected per fold backtest honestly answers "given data available fold, regime structure picked?" Use regime_at() want fixed regime tested across folds.

title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
code: regime_spec(...)

title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
text: ... kwargs passed verbatim detect_regime() spec invoked (e.g. target, , min_run, method).

title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
text: function one argument ("Triangle") returning "Regime" object. caller decides triangle pass (full vs. masked); inside backtest() always masked training triangle.

title: Build a lazy regime detection spec — regime_spec
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Capture detection arguments, defer execution until fit time. spec <- regime_spec(target = "loss_ata")  # In fit_lr(): closure is invoked on the user's `tri`. fit <- fit_lr(tri, loss_regime = regime_spec(target = "loss_ata"))  # In backtest(): closure is invoked on the *masked* triangle of # each holdout fold, so detected change points never peek at # held-out cells. bt <- backtest(tri, holdout = 6L,                loss_regime = regime_spec(target = "loss_ata")) } # }

what: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
text: Check (groups, cohort) cohort consecutive dev sequence within observed range. Non-consecutive cohorts produce non-consecutive age--age links downstream (e.g., 14 -> 17 instead 14 -> 15), breaks summary.Link() key uniqueness causes cartesian joins fit_lr(). function inspects raw data without modifying . Use as_triangle() decide whether fix data source, drop offending cohorts, pass fill_gaps = TRUE as_triangle(). Two checks performed: Cohort dev-sequence gaps – (group, cohort), report missing dev values within observed range. Row-level calendar consistency – calendar supplied, report rows calendar < cohort. rows logically impossible (claims precede policy issue) downstream show negative dev_m, polluting cohort dev sequences.

title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
code: validate_triangle(   df,   groups = NULL,   cohort,   calendar = NULL,   development = NULL,   grain = "auto" )

title: Validate triangle structure before building a development — validate_triangle
text: df data.frame. groups Grouping variable(s). cohort single cohort variable (raw column name). calendar Optional calendar period variable row-level consistency check. supplied, rows calendar < cohort flagged invalid. Default NULL (skip check). development single development-period variable (raw column name). Optional calendar supplied – dev derived (cohort, calendar) resolved grain (dispatch as_triangle()). grain Grain string ("M" / "Q" / "H" / "Y") "auto" (default) – used development derived (cohort, calendar). Ignored development supplied.

Columns: groups, cohort Cohort identifier. n_dev Number distinct observed dev values. n_expected max(dev) - min(dev) + 1 cohort. missing List column missing dev values. Returns zero-row data.table gaps found. Row-level violations (calendar supplied check finds ) attached "invalid_rows" attribute – data.table columns [groups, cohort, calendar, dev (present), reason]. Use attr(, "invalid_rows") rely print.TriangleValidation displays sections.

title: lossratio (development version)
text: BREAKING — four constructor functions renamed build_* as_* align tidyverse coercion idiom Python sibling’s lr.Triangle(df) mental model: build_triangle() -> as_triangle() build_calendar() -> as_calendar() build_total() -> as_total() build_link() -> as_link() signature change, verb. Migration global find--replace. functions still validate, coerce, aggregate substantively – as_* name reflects returned object canonical lossratio shape derived raw experience data, just thin type cast. PascalCase classes (Triangle, Calendar, Total, Link) remain unchanged collide ChainLadder’s lowercase triangle class. BREAKING — plot_triangle.Triangle() argument type renamed view parity plot_triangle.CLFit(), plot_triangle.LRFit(), plot_triangle.Backtest(), already used view = c("value", "usage"). type = slot left free plot-method-specific semantics (plot.Backtest(type = "col"/"diag"/"cell"), plot.CLFit(type = "projection"/"reserve"), etc.). Migration: plot_triangle(tri, type = "usage") -> plot_triangle(tri, view = "usage"). Known limitation (future work) — segment_wise mini-triangle gap-fill. treatment = "segment_wise", fit_* produces mini-triangle per regime segment using segment’s cohorts; cells fall outside every mini-triangle (typically old cohorts late development periods reached single segment) currently left unprojected. follow-phase add fallback (e.g. latest-segment factor, previous-segment factor, smoothing across segments) controlled Regime$fallback knob, plus warning k*k^* exceeds change--now horizon latest segment (also cause mini-triangle truncated). Today cells render unused plot_triangle(view = "usage") rather filled inferred projection. BREAKING — backtest() cell-level columns renamed target_actual / target_proj (_incr siblings) actual / expected (actual_incr / expected_incr). new names match actuarial /E convention (aeg = actual - expected, ae_err = actual / expected - 1) self-document role column. Worker-layer column names (target_proj etc. CLFit$full / EDFit$full) unchanged – rename scoped backtest$ae_err. Migration: replace bt$ae_err$target_actual bt$ae_err$actual, bt$ae_err$target_proj bt$ae_err$expected. backtest() result slot fit_fn_name renamed dispatcher clarity (value still dispatcher name — fit_lr / fit_loss / fit_premium — selected target=). print() / summary() labels updated accordingly. fit_loss(), fit_premium(), fit_lr(), backtest() now attach $usage data.table result: one row per (group, cohort, dev) cell pre-filter triangle status factor (used / unused / holdout / future). plot_triangle(fit, view = "usage") reads directly instead re-deriving filter logic plot time, heatmap always matches cells fit actually saw. New internal helper .build_usage() packages 2-pass maturity detection plus .compute_triangle_usage() attaches filter metadata (regime / recent / holdout / m_k / m_k_dt) data.table attributes renderer. BREAKING — build_triangle(), build_total(), validate_triangle() rename dev = argument development =. new name explicit development-period axis (matching coh <- cohort symmetry inside function bodies). Migration: replace build_triangle(..., dev = "dev_m") build_triangle(..., development = "dev_m"). BREAKING — backtest() result columns renamed value_proj / value_actual (_incr variants) target_proj / target_actual (_incr variants), matching worker-layer target_* generic convention. BREAKING — summary column renames <metric>_<stat> consistency: summary.LossFit / summary.PremiumFit: se_ultimate / cv_ultimate -> ultimate_se / ultimate_cv. .compute_dv() output: median_lr / mad_lr -> lr_median / lr_mad. Internal var_lr (LR variance scratch column) -> lr_var. detect_regime_optimal_window() diagnostics column: mean_magnitude -> magnitude_mean. plot_triangle() now derives axis grain via attr(tri, "grain") raw column name (uy_m, cy_q, …) one package-standard forms, user-supplied names like uym elap_m still render tick labels abbreviated format (23.04, 23.1Q, …). plot_triangle(fit, view = "usage") regime / segment_wise routing fixed: per-group dispatch now honours regime$groups even multi_group = FALSE, regime_at(coverage = "SUR", ...) scopes hline / cohort cut SUR facet . Filtered-cells render unused (gray) instead future (white). .datatable.aware <- TRUE declared R/zzz.R; data.table NSE NOTEs suppressed via mlr3-style (".col") := LHS pattern + reorganised globalVariables() list. Internal temp markers (.col prefix) use function-local NULL bindings per data.table official recommendation. BREAKING — as_experience(), check_experience(), is_experience() removed along Experience S3 class. build_triangle() already validates required columns, coerces dates numerics, aggregates inline, explicit coercion step longer needed (class never required downstream function). Migration: replace exp <- as_experience(df); build_triangle(exp, ...) build_triangle(df, ...). Matches Python sibling 0.0.1.dev7. New fit_intensity() + IntensityFit S3 class (R/intensity.R) — factor-level ED diagnostic, parallel fit_ata() multiplicative side. Returns per-link WLS-estimated intensities g_k standard errors diagnostic stats; projection. ED maturity concept, fit_intensity deliberately omits maturity_args. Link cell-level column g renamed intensity (concept-based, parallels ATA’s ata). Summary / fit per-link output columns (g, g_se, g_var, g_selected) keep Mack-style symbol naming parallelism ATA summary’s f, f_se. Layered naming: cell layer uses concept (intensity), summary layer uses symbol (g). backtest(): cell-level metric aggregation columns renamed aeg ae_err (column ae_err, aggregations ae_err_mean / ae_err_med / ae_err_wt). Print plot labels updated “/E Error”. Formula unchanged: (actual - pred) / pred.

title: lossratio 0.0.0.9000
text: Aggregation: build_triangle() (cohort × dev), build_calendar() (calendar period), build_total() (portfolio total). build_triangle() validates schema coerces required columns inline. Link table: build_link() returns Link object covering single-variable (ATA-style) dual-variable (ED-style) workflows. summary.Link(model = "ata"|"ed") dispatches matching diagnostic. Estimation: fit_ata() (per-link factors ); fit_ed(), fit_cl(), fit_lr() (factors + projection). fit_lr supports three methods — "sa" (default), "ed", "cl". Cell-selection diagnostics: detect_maturity() (dev axis — link beyond ATA factors stable), detect_regime() (cohort axis — structural breaks across underwriting cohorts). Projection diagnostic: detect_convergence() (operates fitted LRFit; valuation depth projected ultimate loss ratio stops revising). Backtest: backtest() (calendar-diagonal hold-, supports fit_cl, fit_ed, fit_lr). Visualisation: S3 plot() plot_triangle() methods every fit class.

title: lossratio 0.0.0.9000
text: experience — 2,664-row synthetic example data, generated data-raw/make_experience.R.

title: lossratio 0.0.0.9000
text: Seven vignettes covering getting started, aggregation frameworks, chain ladder, loss-ratio projection methods, triangle / ata diagnostics, regime detection, backtesting.
